📘 simpsons-paradox RuleSpeak
›

Show in this document

📘 simpsons-paradox RuleSpeak

Digital mirror of the Simpson's Paradox domain. The entities are Studies, Treatments, Strata, and CaseCells (raw patient counts). SuccessRates, TreatmentSummaries, and TreatmentRankings are derived. The paradox falls out of the DAG as an emergent derived fact — it is never modeled directly.

Declarative business rules rendered from the rulebook — the single source of truth. Every statement below is the plain-language reading of a formula; nothing here is hand-authored prose.

Use the in the top-right corner to turn each part of the documentation on or off — RuleSpeak, the literal reading, the raw formula, the author's description, and the links to a rule's inputs and consumers. Your choices are remembered.

Loop

A loop is identified by its name.

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Short title for what this loop adds to the domain model.

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

planned | in-progress | complete

● New Concept Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The new first-class domain concept introduced this loop.

● Domain Question Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The natural-language question this loop's concept answers FROM the domain data.

● Mock Data Note Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What the seed data is designed to show.

● Next Suggestion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Suggestion for the next loop, written after witnessing the DAG at this loop's end.

↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-1
In RuleSpeak

A loop's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDLoop FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Loop

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

↗ Tradition Core Conern Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-2
In RuleSpeak

A loop's tradition core conern is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[CoreConcern])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDLoop FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Loop

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId — which intellectual concern drove this loop.

● Commit Hash Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Commit Short Loop

Full git SHA where this loop row first landed in effortless-rulebook.json — the replay anchor for this turn of the Leopold loop.

ƒ Commit Short Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-3
In RuleSpeak

A loop's commit short is computed as the first 7 character(s) of the commit hash.

In plain words

The first 7 characters of commit hash.

As a formula
= LEFT(Commit Hash, 7)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Commit HashLoop Full git SHA where this loop row first landed in effortless-rulebook.json — the replay anchor for this turn of the Leopold loop.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Commit Hash Loop

First 7 characters of CommitHash for display and git checkout.

● Commit Date Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the commit where this loop landed.

● Commit Message Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Git Tag Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Optional milestone tag pointing at this loop's landing commit (sp-loop-milestone-*). Used for discovery replay without memorizing SHAs.

Year the study was published. Used for temporal stratification and pre-registration dating.

Σ Medicine Study Count Model Summary Σ Epidemiology Study Count Model Summary ƒ Economics Study Count Model Summary Σ Current Imported Count Domain Expansion Target Other Domain Study Count Model Summary Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target

Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification.

● Is Synthetic Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 7 direct +10 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Compliance Study Σ Real Study Count Model Summary Σ Medicine Study Count Model Summary Σ Epidemiology Study Count Model Summary ƒ Economics Study Count Model Summary Σ Real Study Count Ingestion Summary Σ Current Imported Count Domain Expansion Target Real Fully Compliant Count Ingestion Summary Other Domain Study Count Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Identity Map Coverage Rate Model Summary All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target

TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-4
In RuleSpeak

A study's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the study.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study ID.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study ID)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ●Study IDStudy Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Study ID Study

Total number of cases (trials) across all CaseCells in this study.

Σ Cell Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-5
In RuleSpeak

A study's cell count is the number of case cells related to the study.

In plain words

Count of case cells whose study is study ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ●Study IDStudy Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Cell Parity Study Ingestion Compliance Study Structural Compliant Count Ingestion Summary Real Fully Compliant Count Ingestion Summary All Studies Structural Ingestion Summary All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Case Cell ● Study ID Study

Number of CaseCell rows for this study.

ƒ Ingestion Cell Parity Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-18
In RuleSpeak

A study is flagged ingestion cell parity if the value of AND(LOOKUP(StudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, CellCount = LOOKUP(StudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).

In plain words

AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).

As a formula
= AND(LOOKUP(Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, Cell Count = LOOKUP(Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study IDStudy Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973). ΣCell CountStudy Count of case cells whose study is study ID.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Compliance Study Σ Structural Compliant Count Ingestion Summary Real Fully Compliant Count Ingestion Summary All Studies Structural Ingestion Summary All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell

TRUE when CellCount = 2 x StratumCount.

ƒ Ingestion Compliance Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-19
In RuleSpeak

A study's ingestion compliance is computed as the value of IF(IsSynthetic, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND(IngestionCellParity, LOOKUP(StudyId, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).

In plain words

IF({{IsSynthetic}}, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND({{IngestionCellParity}}, LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).

As a formula
= IF(Is Synthetic, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND(Ingestion Cell Parity, LOOKUP(Study ID, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial"))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims. ƒIngestion Cell ParityStudy AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2). ●Study IDStudy Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Real Fully Compliant Count Ingestion Summary All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell

all | partial | exempt-synthetic.

● Is Control Study Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 4 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Study Count Ingestion Summary Σ Real Study Count Ingestion Summary Σ Simpson Study Count Corpus Balance Σ Control Study Count Corpus Balance All Studies Structural Ingestion Summary All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Total Study Count Corpus Balance Control Fraction Corpus Balance Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary

TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.

● Control Domain Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Domain slug for control studies (e.g. medicine-rct, education-higher). NULL for non-control studies.

● Valid Time Start Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Valid Time Midpoint Study Valid Time Decade Study

First year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known.

● Valid Time End Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Valid Time Midpoint Study Valid Time Decade Study

Last year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known.

ƒ Valid Time Midpoint Calculated 1 hop from ground truth float DR-20
In RuleSpeak

The study's valid time midpoint is determined by the following priority:

  1. the valid time start plus the valid time end divided by 2 if all of these hold:
    • the valid time start has a value
    • the valid time end has a value
  2. the publication year in all other cases
In plain words

If valid time start is not "" and valid time end is not "" then ((valid time start plus valid time end) divided by 2), otherwise publication year.

As a formula
= IF(AND(Valid Time Start <> "", Valid Time End <> ""), (Valid Time Start + Valid Time End) / 2, Publication Year)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Valid Time StartStudy First year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known. ●Valid Time EndStudy Last year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known. ●Publication YearStudy Year the study was published. Used for temporal stratification and pre-registration dating.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Valid Time Decade Study
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Valid Time Start Study ● Valid Time End Study ● Publication Year Study

Midpoint year of the valid-time cohort window, used to bucket studies by when their underlying data actually occurred rather than when the paper was published.

ƒ Valid Time Decade Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-21
In RuleSpeak

The study's valid time decade is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the valid time midpoint is blank
  2. the valid time midpoint divided by 10 rounded down to a whole number times 10 in all other cases
In plain words

If valid time midpoint is "" then "", otherwise ((valid time midpoint divided by 10) rounded down times 10).

As a formula
= IF(Valid Time Midpoint = "", "", FLOOR(Valid Time Midpoint / 10) * 10)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒValid Time MidpointStudy If valid time start is not "" and valid time end is not "" then ((valid time start plus valid time end) divided by 2), otherwise publication year.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Valid Time Start Study ● Valid Time End Study ● Publication Year Study

ValidTimeMidpoint floored to the decade (e.g. 1973 -> 1970). The bucket key ConfounderDistortionTimeline aggregates over.

Treatment

A treatment is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Total Cases Treatment Σ Total Successes Treatment Pooled Success Rate Treatment

FK to the parent Study.

● Treatment Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Total Cases Treatment Σ Total Successes Treatment Pooled Success Rate Treatment

Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-language description of what this treatment is.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-22
In RuleSpeak

A treatment's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the treatment.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelTreatment Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Pooled Success Rate Treatment
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Treatment

Total cases for this treatment across all strata.

Σ Total Successes Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-23
In RuleSpeak

A treatment's total successes is the total successes across the case cells related to the treatment.

In plain words

Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Successes, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●SuccessesCase Cell Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelTreatment Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Pooled Success Rate Treatment
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Treatment

Total successes for this treatment across all strata.

ƒ Pooled Success Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-24
In RuleSpeak

The treatment's pooled success rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases is 0
  2. the total successes divided by the total cases in all other cases
In plain words

If total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes divided by total cases).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases = 0, "", Total Successes / Total Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal CasesTreatment Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label. ΣTotal SuccessesTreatment Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Treatment ● Successes Case Cell

Pooled (across all strata) success rate for this treatment: TotalSuccesses / TotalCases.

A strata is identified by its name and is related to a study.

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-language description of what defines this stratum.

Σ Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-25
In RuleSpeak

A strata's total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the strata.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and stratum label is stratum label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStrata FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCase Cell The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStrata Short label used in CaseCells (e.g. small, large, dept-A).

Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived.

ƒ Cell Success Rate Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-26
In RuleSpeak

The case cell's cell success rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the cases is 0
  2. the successes divided by the cases in all other cases
In plain words

If cases equals 0 then 0, otherwise (successes divided by cases).

As a formula
= IF(Cases = 0, 0, Successes / Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ●SuccessesCase Cell Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

Success rate within this cell: Successes / Cases. The first derived fact in the DAG. Fixed loop-90: this rulebook's IF(x=0,"",...) idiom transpiles to a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime for the one CaseCells row with Cases=0 (surfaced only once _erb_tr_metrics stopped masking it). Returns 0 for the degenerate zero-cases cell instead.

Σ Total Cases for Treatment Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-27
In RuleSpeak

A case cell's total cases for treatment is the total cases across the case cells related to the case cell.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Treatment Exposure Fraction Case Cell
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell

Total cases across ALL strata for this treatment in this study — the denominator for TreatmentExposureFraction.

ƒ Treatment Exposure Fraction Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-28
In RuleSpeak

The case cell's treatment exposure fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases for treatment is 0
  2. the cases divided by the total cases for treatment in all other cases
In plain words

If total cases for treatment equals 0 then "", otherwise (cases divided by total cases for treatment).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases for Treatment = 0, "", Cases / Total Cases for Treatment)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Cases for TreatmentCase Cell Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment label. ●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived.
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell

Fraction of this treatment's total cases that fall in this stratum: Cases / TotalCasesForTreatment. High imbalance across strata is the mechanism of confounding — when treatments are allocated very differently across strata, the pooled rate conflates treatment effect with stratum difficulty.

ƒ Is Valid Ingestion Cell Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-29
In RuleSpeak

A case cell is considered a valid ingestion cell if all of the following:

    • the cases is at least 0
    • the successes is at least 0
    • the successes is at most the cases
In plain words

Cases is at least 0, successes is at least 0, and successes is at most cases.

As a formula
= AND(Cases >= 0, Successes >= 0, Successes <= Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ●SuccessesCase Cell Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Valid Cell Count Ingestion Summary All Cells Valid Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

TRUE when Cases>=0, Successes>=0, and Successes<=Cases (loop-92: a stratum-treatment combination can legitimately have zero observed cases in real source data -- e.g. rdatasets-mtcars-engine has zero 8-cylinder straight-engine cars -- so Cases=0 is a valid ingestion cell, not a defect).

Stratum Summary

A stratum summary is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

Σ Stratum Successes Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-30
In RuleSpeak

A stratum summary's stratum successes is the total successes across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

In plain words

Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Successes, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 7 fields this rule reads
●SuccessesCase Cell Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCase Cell The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratum Summary Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Stratum Success Rate Stratum Summary Weighted Stratum Rate Stratum Summary Pooled Rate From Weights a Treatment Ranking Pooled Rate From Weights B Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

Total successes in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

Σ Stratum Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-31
In RuleSpeak

A stratum summary's stratum cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, Treatment Label)
Inputs 7 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCase Cell The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratum Summary Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Stratum Success Rate Stratum Summary Weighted Stratum Rate Stratum Summary Pooled Rate From Weights a Treatment Ranking Pooled Rate From Weights B Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

Total cases in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

ƒ Stratum Success Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-32
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's stratum success rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum cases is 0
  2. the stratum successes divided by the stratum cases in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes divided by stratum cases).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Cases = 0, "", Stratum Successes / Stratum Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣStratum CasesStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label. ΣStratum SuccessesStratum Summary Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is treatment label.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Weighted Stratum Rate Stratum Summary Pooled Rate From Weights a Treatment Ranking Pooled Rate From Weights B Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Successes Case Cell

Success rate within this stratum for this treatment: StratumSuccesses / StratumCases. The true per-stratum picture.

The stratum summary's stratum rate a is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum cases a is 0
  2. the stratum successes a divided by the stratum cases a in all other cases

If stratum cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes a divided by stratum cases a).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Cases a = 0, "", Stratum Successes a / Stratum Cases a)

Success rate for treatment B in this stratum.

ƒ Stratum Winner Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-39
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's stratum winner is determined by the following priority:

  1. “A” if the stratum rate a is greater than the stratum rate b
  2. “B” in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B".

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Rate a > Stratum Rate B, "A", "B")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStratum Rate aStratum Summary If stratum cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes a divided by stratum cases a). ƒStratum Rate BStratum Summary If stratum cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes b divided by stratum cases b).
Consumers 2 direct +45 transitive rules that read this one

Which treatment wins in this stratum: 'A' if StratumRateA > StratumRateB, else 'B'. Used by TreatmentRankings to detect paradox.

Σ Study Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-40
In RuleSpeak

A stratum summary's study total cases is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study.

Σ Stratum Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-41
In RuleSpeak

In plain words

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label)

● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Total cases in this stratum across both treatments (A + B combined). Numerator for StratumFraction.

ƒ Stratum Fraction Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-42
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's stratum fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the study total cases is 0
  2. the stratum total cases divided by the study total cases in all other cases
In plain words

If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases).

As a formula
= IF(Study Total Cases = 0, "", Stratum Total Cases / Study Total Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣStudy Total CasesStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study. ΣStratum Total CasesStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and stratum label is stratum label.

Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Fraction of the study's total cases that fall in this stratum: StratumTotalCases / StudyTotalCases. The weight the confounder assigns to each stratum. A large StratumFraction on a low-success stratum pulls the pooled rate down; when that stratum is also over-allocated to one treatment, it creates the reversal.

ƒ Weighted Stratum Rate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-43
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's weighted stratum rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum fraction is blank
  2. the stratum fraction times the stratum success rate in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Fraction = "", "", Stratum Fraction * Stratum Success Rate)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStratum FractionStratum Summary If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases). ƒStratum Success RateStratum Summary If stratum cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes divided by stratum cases).
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
Σ Pooled Rate From Weights a Treatment Ranking Σ Pooled Rate From Weights B Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Successes Case Cell

StratumFraction × StratumSuccessRate. The contribution of this (stratum, treatment) row to the study-level pooled rate. Summing WeightedStratumRate across all strata for a given treatment reconstructs that treatment's pooled rate from stratum weights — witnessing the mechanism equation.

Σ Treatment a Cases Here Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-44
In RuleSpeak

A stratum summary's treatment a cases here is the total cases across the case cells related to the stratum summary.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "A".

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Stratum Label, Stratum Label, Treatment Label, "A")
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ●Stratum LabelCase Cell The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Stratum LabelStratum Summary Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel). ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

Cases for treatment A in this stratum — used to compute TreatmentA's allocation fraction in this stratum.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is "A".

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "A")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads

Σ Treatment B Total Cases Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-47
In RuleSpeak

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is "B".

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "B")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
●CasesCase Cell Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived. ⇢StudyCase Cell FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelCase Cell The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

Total cases for treatment B across all strata in this study — denominator for AllocationFractionB.

ƒ Allocation Fraction a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-48
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's allocation fraction a is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the treatment a total cases is 0
  2. the treatment a cases here divided by the treatment a total cases in all other cases
In plain words

If treatment a total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment a cases here divided by treatment a total cases).

As a formula
= IF(Treatment a Total Cases = 0, "", Treatment a Cases Here / Treatment a Total Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTreatment a Total CasesStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is "A". ΣTreatment a Cases HereStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "A".
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Allocation Bias Stratum Summary Abs Allocation Bias Stratum Summary Max Stratum Imbalance Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Fraction of treatment A's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentACasesHere / TreatmentATotalCases. Equivalent to TreatmentExposureFraction for A.

ƒ Allocation Fraction B Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-49
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's allocation fraction b is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the treatment b total cases is 0
  2. the treatment b cases here divided by the treatment b total cases in all other cases
In plain words

If treatment b total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment b cases here divided by treatment b total cases).

As a formula
= IF(Treatment B Total Cases = 0, "", Treatment B Cases Here / Treatment B Total Cases)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTreatment B Total CasesStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is "B". ΣTreatment B Cases HereStratum Summary Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study, stratum label is stratum label, and treatment label is "B".
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Allocation Bias Stratum Summary Abs Allocation Bias Stratum Summary Max Stratum Imbalance Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Fraction of treatment B's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentBCasesHere / TreatmentBTotalCases.

ƒ Allocation Bias Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-50
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's allocation bias is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the allocation fraction a is blank
  2. the allocation fraction a minus the allocation fraction b in all other cases
In plain words

If allocation fraction a is "" then "", otherwise (allocation fraction a minus allocation fraction b).

As a formula
= IF(Allocation Fraction a = "", "", Allocation Fraction a - Allocation Fraction B)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation Fraction aStratum Summary If treatment a total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment a cases here divided by treatment a total cases). ƒAllocation Fraction BStratum Summary If treatment b total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (treatment b cases here divided by treatment b total cases).
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Abs Allocation Bias Stratum Summary Max Stratum Imbalance Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

AllocationFractionA minus AllocationFractionB: how much more of treatment A (relative to B) is concentrated in this stratum. Positive = A is over-allocated here; negative = B is over-allocated here. When AllocationBias is large and negative in a low-success stratum, treatment A is under-represented in the hard cases — making A look worse in the pooled view than it is in any stratum. This is the confounder's fingerprint as a signed number.

ƒ Stratum Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-51
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's stratum gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum rate a is blank
  2. the stratum rate a minus the stratum rate b in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum rate a is "" then "", otherwise (stratum rate a minus stratum rate b).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Rate a = "", "", Stratum Rate a - Stratum Rate B)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStratum Rate aStratum Summary If stratum cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes a divided by stratum cases a). ƒStratum Rate BStratum Summary If stratum cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum successes b divided by stratum cases b).
Consumers 2 direct +73 transitive rules that read this one

Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Signal Purity Gap Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

Signed rate difference within this stratum: StratumRateA minus StratumRateB. Positive = A wins here; negative = B wins here; zero = tied. When every stratum has StratumGap > 0 but the pooled gap is negative, the contradiction IS Simpson's Paradox made arithmetically visible in one column. Multiply StratumGap × StratumFraction and sum across strata to get the equal-weight pooled gap (the signal allocation would produce without confounding).

ƒ Weighted Stratum Gap Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-52
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's weighted stratum gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum gap is blank
  2. the stratum gap times the stratum fraction in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise (stratum gap times stratum fraction).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Gap = "", "", Stratum Gap * Stratum Fraction)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStratum GapStratum Summary If stratum rate a is "" then "", otherwise (stratum rate a minus stratum rate b). ƒStratum FractionStratum Summary If study total cases equals 0 then "", otherwise (stratum total cases divided by study total cases).
Consumers 1 direct +71 transitive rules that read this one
