Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

StratumGap × StratumFraction: the contribution of this stratum's treatment-rate difference to the equal-weight pooled gap. Summing WeightedStratumGap across all TreatmentA rows for a study gives WeightedStratumGapSum — the pooled gap the study would show under balanced allocation. This is the per-stratum building block of the sign-flip test.

ƒ Abs Allocation Bias Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-53
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's abs allocation bias is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the allocation bias is blank
  2. the absolute value of the allocation bias in all other cases
In plain words

If allocation bias is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of allocation bias.

As a formula
= IF(Allocation Bias = "", "", ABS(Allocation Bias))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation BiasStratum Summary If allocation fraction a is "" then "", otherwise (allocation fraction a minus allocation fraction b).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
Σ Max Stratum Imbalance Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

ABS(AllocationBias). MAXIFS-friendly unsigned magnitude so TreatmentRankings.MaxStratumImbalance can compute live via MAXIFS instead of a hand-written cache.

ƒ Abs Stratum Gap Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-54
In RuleSpeak

The stratum summary's abs stratum gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum gap is blank
  2. the absolute value of the stratum gap in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of stratum gap.

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Gap = "", "", ABS(Stratum Gap))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒStratum GapStratum Summary If stratum rate a is "" then "", otherwise (stratum rate a minus stratum rate b).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
Σ Max Stratum Gap Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

ABS(StratumGap). MAXIFS-friendly unsigned magnitude so TreatmentRankings.MaxStratumGap can compute live via MAXIFS instead of a hand-written cache.

Model Summary

A model summary is identified by its name.

Σ Reversal Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-55
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's reversal count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is reversal is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Reversal, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ReversalTreatment Ranking If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Study Count Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of studies with IsReversal=TRUE (sign-flip / allocation-driven reversal).

Σ Non Reversal Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-56
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's non reversal count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is reversal is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Reversal, FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ReversalTreatment Ranking If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Study Count Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of studies with IsReversal=FALSE.

ƒ Study Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-57
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's study count is computed as the reversal count plus the non reversal count.

In plain words

Reversal count plus non reversal count.

As a formula
= Reversal Count + Non Reversal Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣReversal CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose is reversal is true(). ΣNon Reversal CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose is reversal is false().
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary ƒ Type a Fraction Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Total number of TreatmentRankings in this model: ReversalCount + NonReversalCount.

Σ Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-58
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's explained count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is paradox explained is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatment Ranking Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Number of studies with IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (reversal AND confirmed confounder).

Σ Total Paradox Strength Aggregation 7 hops from ground truth number DR-59
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's total paradox strength is the total paradox strength across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Sum of paradox strength across all treatment rankings whose treatment a is "A".

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Paradox Strength, Treatment a, "A")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒParadox StrengthTreatment Ranking If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (pooled gap times (strata won by loser divided by stratum count)). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Sum of ParadoxStrength across all studies. Used to compute average.

ƒ Avg Paradox Strength Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-60
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's avg paradox strength is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the study count is 0
  2. the total paradox strength divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (total paradox strength divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(Study Count = 0, "", Total Paradox Strength / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy CountModel Summary Reversal count plus non reversal count. ΣTotal Paradox StrengthModel Summary Sum of paradox strength across all treatment rankings whose treatment a is "A".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Average ParadoxStrength across all studies: TotalParadoxStrength / StudyCount. A scalar summary of how paradox-rich this dataset is.

Σ Type a Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-61
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type a count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "A".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "A")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Type a Fraction Model Summary ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies classified as Type-A: sign flip AND full reversal (ReversalIntensity=1). The canonical Simpson's Paradox — allocation distortion reverses the pooled winner.

Σ Danger Tier Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-62
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's danger tier count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose screening tier is "DANGER".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Screening Tier, "DANGER")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒScreening TierTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "A" or distortion type is "B" then "DANGER", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "CAUTION", otherwise if distortion type is "D" then "SAFE", otherwise "".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies where TreatmentRankings.ScreeningTier = DANGER — pooled sign is wrong (DistortionType A or B).

Σ Caution Tier Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-63
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's caution tier count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose screening tier is "CAUTION".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Screening Tier, "CAUTION")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒScreening TierTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "A" or distortion type is "B" then "DANGER", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "CAUTION", otherwise if distortion type is "D" then "SAFE", otherwise "".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies where TreatmentRankings.ScreeningTier = CAUTION — pooled sign holds but magnitude is distorted (DistortionType C+ or C-).

Σ Safe Tier Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-64
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's safe tier count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose screening tier is "SAFE".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Screening Tier, "SAFE")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒScreening TierTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "A" or distortion type is "B" then "DANGER", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "CAUTION", otherwise if distortion type is "D" then "SAFE", otherwise "".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies where TreatmentRankings.ScreeningTier = SAFE — pooling is trustworthy (DistortionType D).

Σ Type B Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-65
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type b count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "B".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "B")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies classified as Type-B: sign flip but partial reversal (ReversalIntensity<1). Allocation distortion creates a sign flip but the pooled signal partially reflects per-stratum reality.

Σ Type D Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-66
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type d count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "D")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary ƒ Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Studies classified as Type-D: no sign flip, negligible distortion. Pooled analysis is trustworthy — allocation is sufficiently balanced.

ƒ Type a Fraction Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-67
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's type a fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the study count is 0
  2. the type a count divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type a count divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(Study Count = 0, "", Type a Count / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy CountModel Summary Reversal count plus non reversal count. ΣType a CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "A".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Fraction of studies that are Type-A (full canonical reversals). TypeACount / StudyCount.

ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Calculated 9 hops from ground truth string DR-68
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's distortion taxonomy coverage is computed as “A:”, followed by the type a count, followed by “ B:”, followed by the type b count, followed by “ C+:”, followed by the c amplification count, followed by “ C-:”, followed by the c compression count, followed by “ D:”, followed by the type d count.

In plain words

"A:", type a count, " B:", type b count, " C+:", c amplification count, " C-:", c compression count, " D:", joined with type d count.

As a formula
= CONCAT("A:", Type a Count, " B:", Type B Count, " C+:", C Amplification Count, " C-:", C Compression Count, " D:", Type D Count)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ΣType a CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "A". ΣType B CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "B". ΣC Amplification CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C+". ΣC Compression CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C-". ΣType D CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Human-readable summary of the four-type distribution: e.g. 'A:2 B:1 C:1 D:0'. The model's self-portrait of its own distortion geometry.

ƒ Distortion Only Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-69
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's distortion only count is computed as the c amplification count plus the c compression count.

In plain words

C amplification count plus c compression count.

As a formula
= C Amplification Count + C Compression Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣC Amplification CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C+". ΣC Compression CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C-".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of studies with real allocation distortion but no sign flip: TypeCCount. The pooled winner is correct but the effect size is biased. Distinct from ZeroStrengthCount, which conflates Type-C (real distortion) with Type-D (neutral).

Σ C Amplification Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-70
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's c amplification count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C+".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "C+")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary ƒ Distortion Only Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of studies classified as type C+ (allocation-amplified): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap overstates true advantage. Causes researcher overconfidence — may accept higher cost/risk than true effect warrants.

Σ C Compression Count Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth integer DR-71
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's c compression count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C-".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "C-")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary ƒ Distortion Only Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of studies classified as type C- (allocation-compressed): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap understates true advantage. Causes researcher underconfidence — effective treatment may be prematurely abandoned.

Σ Avg Signal Purity Aggregation 8 hops from ground truth number DR-72
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's avg signal purity is an aggregated value computed across the model summary's related records.

In plain words

The sum of signal purity divided by count(treatment ranking ID).

As a formula
= SUM(Signal Purity) / COUNT(Treatment Ranking ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSignal PurityTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ●Treatment Ranking IDTreatment Ranking Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-A-vs-B).
Ground truth at the leaves 12 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Ranking ID Treatment Ranking

Average SignalPurity across all TreatmentRankings. Below 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal on average — the corpus is predominantly misleading at the pooled level. Above 0.5 means the corpus is predominantly trustworthy. Fixed loop-90: COUNTIFS(...,"<>")/SUMIFS(...,"<>") mistranslate to a literal '<>' string-equality filter (a known transpiler bug), always returning 0/NULL; replaced with SUM/COUNT, both of which transpile correctly.

Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Max Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-73
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sweep corrected gap max is the largest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the model summary.

In plain words

Largest sweep corrected gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986".

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMin — the invariant.

Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Min Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth number DR-74
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sweep corrected gap min is the smallest sweep corrected gap across the allocation sweep related to the model summary.

In plain words

Smallest sweep corrected gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986".

As a formula
= MINIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]). ●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMax — the invariant.

ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Range Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-75
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's sweep corrected gap range is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the sweep corrected gap max is blank
  2. the sweep corrected gap max minus the sweep corrected gap min in all other cases
In plain words

If sweep corrected gap max is "" then "", otherwise (sweep corrected gap max minus sweep corrected gap min).

As a formula
= IF(Sweep Corrected Gap Max = "", "", Sweep Corrected Gap Max - Sweep Corrected Gap Min)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣSweep Corrected Gap MaxModel Summary Largest sweep corrected gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986". ΣSweep Corrected Gap MinModel Summary Smallest sweep corrected gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986".
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

SweepCorrectedGapMax − SweepCorrectedGapMin. The allocation-invariance witness: this should be zero (up to floating-point rounding). If nonzero, the corrected gap is not allocation-free.

Σ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Aggregation 6 hops from ground truth number DR-76
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sweep pooled gap range is an aggregated value computed across the model summary's related records.

In plain words

Largest sweep pooled gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986" minus smallest sweep pooled gap across all allocation sweep whose study ID is "kidney-1986".

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Sweep Pooled Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986") - MINIFS(Sweep Pooled Gap, Study ID, "kidney-1986")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Pooled GapAllocation Sweep If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b). ●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

MAX(SweepPooledGap) − MIN(SweepPooledGap) across all AllocationSweep rows. Expected to be large (≈0.21) — the pooled gap wanders as allocation shifts.

Σ Real Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-77
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's real study count is the number of studies related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of studies whose is synthetic is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Synthetic, FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Other Domain Study Count Model Summary ƒ Domain Diversity Note Model Summary ƒ Identity Map Coverage Rate Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study

Number of real (non-synthetic) published studies in the corpus. Retyped loop-90: was mis-declared raw with a formula, which is why the transpiler emitted no native function and 02b had to hand-write calc_model_summary_real_study_count.

ƒ Avg Signal Purity Reversal Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-78
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's avg signal purity reversal is the average signal purity across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average signal purity across all treatment rankings whose allocation direction is "reversal".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Signal Purity, Allocation Direction, "reversal")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSignal PurityTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒAllocation DirectionTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then "reversal", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "amplification", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "compression", otherwise "neutral".
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Signal Purity Gap Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Average SignalPurity across reversal studies (type A and B). Theorem predicts this must be < 0.5. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); rewritten from SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division to AVERAGEIFS because the SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division form transpiled with a mangled cross-table alias.

ƒ Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-79
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's avg signal purity non reversal is the average signal purity across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average signal purity across all treatment rankings whose allocation direction is "<>reversal".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Signal Purity, Allocation Direction, "<>reversal")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSignal PurityTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒAllocation DirectionTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then "reversal", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "amplification", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "compression", otherwise "neutral".
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Signal Purity Gap Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Average SignalPurity across non-reversal studies (type C+, C-, D). Theorem predicts this must be >= 0.5 except edge cases. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); rewritten from SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division to AVERAGEIFS (see AvgSignalPurityReversal).

ƒ Signal Purity Gap Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-80
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's signal purity gap is computed as the avg signal purity non reversal minus the avg signal purity reversal.

In plain words

Avg signal purity non reversal minus avg signal purity reversal.

As a formula
= Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal - Avg Signal Purity Reversal
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAvg Signal Purity Non ReversalModel Summary Average signal purity across all treatment rankings whose allocation direction is "<>reversal". ƒAvg Signal Purity ReversalModel Summary Average signal purity across all treatment rankings whose allocation direction is "reversal".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Separation between reversal and non-reversal average SignalPurity. The larger this gap, the more discriminating the threshold at 0.5. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

Σ Medicine Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-81
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's medicine study count is the number of the model summary's studies that are not synthetic.

In plain words

Count of studies whose domain is "medicine" and is synthetic is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Domain, "medicine", Is Synthetic, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●DomainStudy Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Other Domain Study Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Is Synthetic Study

Real published studies in the medicine domain. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

Σ Epidemiology Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-82
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's epidemiology study count is the number of the model summary's studies that are not synthetic.

In plain words

Count of studies whose domain is "epidemiology" and is synthetic is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Domain, "epidemiology", Is Synthetic, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●DomainStudy Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Other Domain Study Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Is Synthetic Study

Real published studies in the epidemiology domain. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

ƒ Other Domain Study Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-83
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's other domain study count is computed as the real study count minus the medicine study count minus the epidemiology study count.

In plain words

(real study count minus medicine study count) minus epidemiology study count.

As a formula
= Real Study Count - Medicine Study Count - Epidemiology Study Count
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣReal Study CountModel Summary Count of studies whose is synthetic is false(). ΣMedicine Study CountModel Summary Count of studies whose domain is "medicine" and is synthetic is false(). ΣEpidemiology Study CountModel Summary Count of studies whose domain is "epidemiology" and is synthetic is false().
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Domain Study

Real published studies outside medicine and epidemiology (sports, legal, education, social-science). Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

ƒ Domain Diversity Note Calculated 9 hops from ground truth string DR-84
In RuleSpeak ⚠︎ mechanical

A model summary's domain diversity note is computed as “real=”, followed by the real study count, followed by “ studies; wave2=”, followed by the expansion wave2 study count, followed by “; eduLatent=”, followed by the education latent fraction times 100 rounded to 0 decimal place(s), followed by “%; sportsLatent=”, followed by the sports latent fraction times 100 rounded to 0 decimal place(s), followed by “%; econFlipRate=”, followed by the economics sign flip rate times 100 rounded to 0 decimal place(s), followed by “%”.

In plain words

"real=", real study count, " studies; wave2=", expansion wave2 study count, "; eduLatent=", (education latent fraction times 100) rounded to 0 decimal places, "%; sportsLatent=", (sports latent fraction times 100) rounded to 0 decimal places, "%; econFlipRate=", (economics sign flip rate times 100) rounded to 0 decimal places, joined with "%".

As a formula
= CONCAT("real=", Real Study Count, " studies; wave2=", Expansion Wave2 Study Count, "; eduLatent=", ROUND(Education Latent Fraction * 100, 0), "%; sportsLatent=", ROUND(Sports Latent Fraction * 100, 0), "%; econFlipRate=", ROUND(Economics Sign Flip Rate * 100, 0), "%")
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ΣReal Study CountModel Summary Count of studies whose is synthetic is false(). ƒExpansion Wave2 Study CountModel Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-2" and is imported is true(). ƒEducation Latent FractionModel Summary If count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education", distortion type is "D", and is latent only flip is true() divided by count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D"). ƒSports Latent FractionModel Summary If count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "sports" and distortion type is "D" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "sports", distortion type is "D", and is latent only flip is true() divided by count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "sports" and distortion type is "D"). ƒEconomics Sign Flip RateModel Summary If count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" and is sign flip is true() divided by count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics").
Ground truth at the leaves 14 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Expansion Wave Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Human-readable domain breakdown. Witnesses corpus diversity — a prerequisite for cross-domain SignalPurity claims. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); TEXT(...,"0%") is not a supported transpiler function, replaced with ROUND(x*100,0) + literal '%'.

● Synthetic Phase Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Number of parameter grid points in SyntheticPhase. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b-customize-data.sql.

● Phase Diagram Complete Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TRUE when all five DistortionType cells are populated in the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

● Phase Taxonomy Coverage Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Type counts across the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

Σ Ingestion Protocol Item Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-85

In plain words

Count(protocol ID).

As a formula
= COUNT(Protocol ID)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Protocol IDIngestion Protocol Unique slug for this contract item.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Protocol ID Ingestion Protocol

Number of rows in IngestionProtocol — the documented contract items. Fixed loop-90 (see IngestionSummary.ProtocolItemCount for why COUNTIFS(...,"<>") is unsafe).

LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionContractPasses]).

LOOKUP("ingestion-v1", IngestionSummary[IngestionSummaryId], IngestionSummary[IngestionWitnessNote]).

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CandidateCount]).

ƒ Latent Type D Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-93
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's latent type d count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose latent flip potential is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Latent Flip Potential, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Type-D studies with LatentFlipPotential=TRUE.

ƒ Stable Type D Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth number DR-94
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's stable type d count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are not latent flip potential.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Distortion Type, "D", Latent Flip Potential, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Type-D studies without latent flip potential (allocation-stable at observed and counterfactual sweep).

ƒ Latent Type D Fraction Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-95
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's latent type d fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the type d count is 0
  2. the latent type d count divided by the type d count in all other cases
In plain words

If type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise (latent type d count divided by type d count).

As a formula
= IF(Type D Count = 0, "", Latent Type D Count / Type D Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣType D CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D". ƒLatent Type D CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose latent flip potential is true().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Fraction of Type-D studies with latent flip potential.

ƒ Cross Zero Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-96
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's cross zero count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose pooled gap crosses zero is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Studies whose pooled gap crosses zero under allocation sweep.

ƒ Sign Flip Signal Purity Max Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-97
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sign flip signal purity max is the largest signal purity across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Largest signal purity across all treatment rankings whose is sign flip is true().

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Signal Purity, Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSignal PurityTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Maximum SignalPurity among sign-flip studies. Must stay below 0.5 for the signal-purity theorem.

ƒ Economics Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-98
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's economics sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" and is sign flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "economics", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Sign-flip count in economics domain.

Σ Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth number DR-99
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sum pooled gap latent d is the total pooled gap across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Sum of pooled gap across all treatment rankings whose latent flip potential is true().

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Pooled Gap, Latent Flip Potential, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

SUM(PooledGap) among Type-D studies with latent flip potential — a bare, unwrapped SUMIFS split out as its own field because nesting a cross-table SUMIFS/AVERAGEIFS as an operand inside another IF/division expression transpiles with a mangled WHERE-clause column alias (e.g. rankings_latent_flip_potential) that errors at runtime; a bare top-level SUMIFS transpiles correctly (loop-90).

ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-100
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's avg pooled gap latent d is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the latent type d count is 0
  2. the sum pooled gap latent d divided by the latent type d count in all other cases
In plain words

If latent type d count equals 0 then 0, otherwise (sum pooled gap latent d divided by latent type d count).

As a formula
= IF(Latent Type D Count = 0, 0, Sum Pooled Gap Latent D / Latent Type D Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒLatent Type D CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose latent flip potential is true(). ΣSum Pooled Gap Latent DModel Summary Sum of pooled gap across all treatment rankings whose latent flip potential is true().
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Average PooledGap among Type-D studies with latent flip potential. Fixed loop-90 (see SumPooledGapLatentD for why the sum is split into its own field).

Σ Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth number DR-101
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sum pooled gap stable d is the total pooled gap across the model summary's treatment rankings that are not latent flip potential.

In plain words

Sum of pooled gap across all treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false().

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Pooled Gap, Distortion Type, "D", Latent Flip Potential, FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒLatent Flip PotentialTreatment Ranking If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

SUM(PooledGap) among allocation-stable Type-D studies — split out as a bare field for the same reason as SumPooledGapLatentD.

ƒ Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Calculated 10 hops from ground truth number DR-102
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's avg pooled gap stable d is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the stable type d count is 0
  2. the sum pooled gap stable d divided by the stable type d count in all other cases
In plain words

If stable type d count equals 0 then 0, otherwise (sum pooled gap stable d divided by stable type d count).

As a formula
= IF(Stable Type D Count = 0, 0, Sum Pooled Gap Stable D / Stable Type D Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStable Type D CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false(). ΣSum Pooled Gap Stable DModel Summary Sum of pooled gap across all treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D" and latent flip potential is false().
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Average PooledGap among allocation-stable Type-D studies. Fixed loop-90 (see SumPooledGapLatentD).

ƒ Epidemiology Avg Distortion Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-103
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's epidemiology avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average allocation distortion across all treatment rankings whose study domain is "epidemiology".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Study Domain, "epidemiology")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]).

In RuleSpeak

A model summary's education avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average allocation distortion across all treatment rankings whose study domain is "education".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Study Domain, "education")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]).

● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Mean AllocationDistortion in education studies.

ƒ Confounder Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-105
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's confounder sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "confounder" and is sign flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Sign-flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

ƒ Confounder Latent Only Count Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-106
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's confounder latent only count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "confounder" and is latent only flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatment Ranking If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Latent-only flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

ƒ Collider Selection Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-107
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's collider selection count is computed as the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary plus the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" plus count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "selection".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider") + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Studies tagged collider or selection on StratumVariables.

ƒ Collider Selection Manifest Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-108
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's collider selection manifest count is computed as the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips plus the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" and is sign flip is true() plus count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "selection" and is sign flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider", Is Sign Flip, TRUE()) + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection", Is Sign Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

In RuleSpeak

A model summary's collider selection latent only count is computed as the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips plus the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "collider" and is latent only flip is true() plus count of treatment rankings whose stratum causal role is "selection" and is latent only flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "collider", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE()) + COUNTIFS(Stratum Causal Role, "selection", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatment Ranking If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().

⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Σ Explained Confounder Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-110
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's explained confounder count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is paradox explained is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatment Ranking Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Count of TreatmentRankings rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should equal ConfounderSignFlipCount).

Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-111
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's contested or mediator explained count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are paradox-explained and are not adjustment appropriate.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is sign flip is true(), is paradox explained is true(), and adjustment appropriate is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Adjustment Appropriate, FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatment Ranking Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0. ƒAdjustment AppropriateTreatment Ranking Mediator risk count equals 0 and causal claim status is "established".
Ground truth at the leaves 16 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable ● Conditioning Risk Stratum Variable

Count of contested or mediator sign-flip rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should be zero).

ƒ Discovery Witness Note Calculated 11 hops from ground truth string DR-112
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's discovery witness note is computed as “sweep: latentD=”, followed by the latent type d fraction, followed by “ purityMax=”, followed by the sign flip signal purity max, followed by “ catalogExact=”, followed by the type prediction match rate, followed by “ identities=”, followed by the confounder identity count.

In plain words

"sweep: latentD=", latent type d fraction, " purityMax=", sign flip signal purity max, " catalogExact=", type prediction match rate, " identities=", joined with confounder identity count.

As a formula
= CONCAT("sweep: latentD=", Latent Type D Fraction, " purityMax=", Sign Flip Signal Purity Max, " catalogExact=", Type Prediction Match Rate, " identities=", Confounder Identity Count)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒLatent Type D FractionModel Summary If type d count equals 0 then "", otherwise (latent type d count divided by type d count). ƒSign Flip Signal Purity MaxModel Summary Largest signal purity across all treatment rankings whose is sign flip is true(). ↗Type Prediction Match RateModel Summary LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]). ΣConfounder Identity CountModel Summary Count(confounder identity ID).
Ground truth at the leaves 12 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Confounder Identity ID Confounder Identity

Research sweep synthesis rollup (loops 61–66).

↗ Type Prediction Match Count Lookup integer DR-113
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type prediction match count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchCount])

Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchCount.

↗ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Lookup integer DR-114
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type prediction mismatch count is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMismatchCount])

Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMismatchCount.

↗ Type Prediction Match Rate Lookup number DR-115
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type prediction match rate is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate])
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Discovery Witness Note Model Summary ƒ Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary

Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchRate.

↗ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Lookup number DR-116
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sign flip prediction match rate is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate])

↗ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Lookup string DR-117
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's catalog prediction witness note is the value of LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogPredictionWitnessNote]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogPredictionWitnessNote]).

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.
