ƒ C Plus Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-118
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's c plus avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average allocation distortion across all treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C+".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "C+")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C+ studies.

ƒ C Minus Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-119
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's c minus avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average allocation distortion across all treatment rankings whose distortion type is "C-".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "C-")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C- studies.

ƒ Type D Avg Distortion Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-120
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's type d avg distortion is the average allocation distortion across the treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Average allocation distortion across all treatment rankings whose distortion type is "D".

As a formula
= AVERAGEIFS(Allocation Distortion, Distortion Type, "D")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-D studies.

ƒ Sweep Fragile Count Calculated 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-121
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's sweep fragile count is the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is sweep fragile is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Sweep Fragile, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Sweep FragileTreatment Ranking Distortion type is "D", signal purity equals 1, allocation distortion equals 0, sweep pooled gap range is greater than 0.3, and allocation fragility is at least 10.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Count of TreatmentRankings with IsSweepFragile=TRUE.

ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-122
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's expansion wave1 economics expected a count is the number of the model summary's candidate study catalog that are imported; have a domain of “economics”; and have a expected distortion type of “A”.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), and expected distortion type is "A".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1", Domain, "economics", Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Distortion Type, "A")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
●Expansion WaveCandidate Study Catalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1. ●DomainCandidate Study Catalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expansion Wave Candidate Study Catalog ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Imported expansion-wave-1 economics catalog rows tagged Expected A.

ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-123
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's expansion wave1 economics expected AD count is the number of the model summary's candidate study catalog that are imported; have a domain of “economics”; have a expected distortion type of “A”; and have a observed distortion type of “D”.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), expected distortion type is "A", and observed distortion type is "D".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1", Domain, "economics", Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Distortion Type, "A", Observed Distortion Type, "D")
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●Expansion WaveCandidate Study Catalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1. ●DomainCandidate Study Catalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown. ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expansion Wave Candidate Study Catalog ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

Expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

ƒ Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-124
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's economics expected a mismatch rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the expansion wave1 economics expected a count is 0
  2. the expansion wave1 economics expected AD count divided by the expansion wave1 economics expected a count in all other cases
In plain words

If expansion wave1 economics expected a count equals 0 then "", otherwise (expansion wave1 economics expected AD count divided by expansion wave1 economics expected a count).

As a formula
= IF(Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count = 0, "", Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count / Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒExpansion Wave1 Economics Expected a CountModel Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), and expected distortion type is "A". ƒExpansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD CountModel Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1", domain is "economics", is imported is true(), expected distortion type is "A", and observed distortion type is "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expansion Wave Candidate Study Catalog ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

Fraction of expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

ƒ Unanimous Sign Flip Count Calculated 7 hops from ground truth integer DR-125
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's unanimous sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are stratum-unanimous.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is sign flip is true() and is stratum unanimous is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Is Stratum Unanimous, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Stratum UnanimousTreatment Ranking Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Sign-flip studies with unanimous stratum direction (IsSignFlip AND IsStratumUnanimous).

Σ Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth number DR-126
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's max study sweep corrected gap range is the largest sweep corrected gap range across the sweep study summary related to the model summary.

In plain words

The largest of sweep corrected gap range.

As a formula
= MAX(Sweep Corrected Gap Range)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Sweep Corrected Gap RangeSweep Study Summary Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = SweepCorrectedGapMax - SweepCorrectedGapMin. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Sweep Corrected Gap Range Sweep Study Summary

MAX(SweepStudySummary.SweepCorrectedGapRange) — must stay below 0.0001 for CorrectedGap invariance theorem.

Σ Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-127
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's corrected gap invariant fail count is the number of sweep study summary related to the model summary.

In plain words

Count of sweep study summary whose sweep corrected gap range is ">=0.0001".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Sweep Corrected Gap Range, ">=0.0001")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Sweep Corrected Gap RangeSweep Study Summary Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = SweepCorrectedGapMax - SweepCorrectedGapMin. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Sweep Corrected Gap Range Sweep Study Summary

Count of SweepStudySummary rows where SweepCorrectedGapRange >= 0.0001.

Σ False Positive Explained Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-128
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's false positive explained count is the number of the model summary's false positive explaineds.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is paradox explained is true() and is sign flip is false() plus count of treatment rankings whose is paradox explained is true(), is sign flip is true(), and stratum causal role is "<>confounder".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Is Sign Flip, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Is Paradox Explained, TRUE(), Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Stratum Causal Role, "<>confounder")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatment Ranking Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0. ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).
Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

IsParadoxExplained=TRUE rows that are not confounder manifest sign-flips — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.

Σ Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Aggregation 9 hops from ground truth integer DR-129
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's unexplained confounder sign flip count is the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are not paradox-explained and have a stratum causal role of “confounder”.

In plain words

Count of treatment rankings whose is sign flip is true(), stratum causal role is "confounder", and is paradox explained is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Sign Flip, TRUE(), Stratum Causal Role, "confounder", Is Paradox Explained, FALSE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ↗Stratum Causal RoleTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]). ƒIs Paradox ExplainedTreatment Ranking Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Confounder sign-flips with IsParadoxExplained=FALSE — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.

ƒ Theorem Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-130
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's theorem count is the number of the model summary's conclusions that have a status of “witnessed”.

In plain words

Count of conclusions whose category is "corpus-theorem" and status is "witnessed".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Category, "corpus-theorem", Status, "witnessed")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●CategoryConclusion domain | instrument | methodology | open-question | corpus-theorem ●StatusConclusion witnessed | planned | open
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Category Conclusion ● Status Conclusion

Count of witnessed Conclusions with Category=corpus-theorem (portfolio rollup for loop-76; category renamed from theorem in loop-91).

ƒ Education Latent Fraction Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-131
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's education latent fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the model summary has no treatment rankings that have a distortion type of “D”
  2. the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are latent only flips and have a distortion type of “D” divided by the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that have a distortion type of “D” in all other cases
In plain words

If count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education", distortion type is "D", and is latent only flip is true() divided by count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "education" and distortion type is "D").

As a formula
= IF(COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D") = 0, "", COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE()) / COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "education", Distortion Type, "D"))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatment Ranking If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Domain Diversity Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Fraction of education Type-D studies with LatentFlipPotential=TRUE.

  1. an empty string if the model summary has no treatment rankings that have a distortion type of “D”

As a formula
= IF(COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "sports", Distortion Type, "D") = 0, "", COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "sports", Distortion Type, "D", Is Latent Only Flip, TRUE()) / COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "sports", Distortion Type, "D"))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒIs Latent Only FlipTreatment Ranking If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Domain Diversity Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

ƒ Economics Sign Flip Rate Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-133
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's economics sign flip rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the model summary has no treatment rankings
  2. the number of the model summary's treatment rankings that are sign flips divided by the number of treatment rankings related to the model summary in all other cases
In plain words

If count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" equals 0 then "", otherwise (count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" and is sign flip is true() divided by count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics").

As a formula
= IF(COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "economics") = 0, "", COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "economics", Is Sign Flip, TRUE()) / COUNTIFS(Study Domain, "economics"))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒStudy DomainTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Domain Diversity Note Model Summary

ƒ Economics Study Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-138
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's economics study count is the number of the model summary's studies that are not synthetic.

In plain words

Count of studies whose domain is "economics" and is synthetic is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Domain, "economics", Is Synthetic, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●DomainStudy Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Is Synthetic Study

Real economics studies in corpus.

ƒ Identity Map Coverage Rate Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-147
In RuleSpeak

The model summary's identity map coverage rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the real study count is 0
  2. the mapped stratum variable count divided by the real study count in all other cases
In plain words

If real study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (mapped stratum variable count divided by real study count).

As a formula
= IF(Real Study Count=0, "", Mapped Stratum Variable Count / Real Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣReal Study CountModel Summary Count of studies whose is synthetic is false(). ΣMapped Stratum Variable CountModel Summary Count(map ID).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Map ID Stratum Variable Identity Map

Fraction of real-study StratumVariables with a ConfounderIdentity map (loop-81 customize SQL).

● Identity Cluster Witness Note Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Loop-80 identity layer witness rollup (customize SQL).

ƒ Geographic Type D Fraction Calculated number DR-154
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's geographic type d fraction is computed as the value of IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])).

In plain words

IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])).

As a formula
= IF(LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])=0, "", LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[TypeDCount]) / LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]))

ƒ Severity Drift Direction Calculated string DR-155
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's severity drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("id-disease-severity", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection])

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-disease-severity (severity-case-mix archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Geographic Drift Direction Calculated string DR-156

A model summary's geographic drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-geographic-composition", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

LOOKUP("id-geographic-composition", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-geographic-composition (geography archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Situational Drift Direction Calculated string DR-157
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's situational drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-situational-context", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("id-situational-context", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("id-situational-context", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection])

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-situational-context (sports-situation archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Age Drift Direction Calculated string DR-158
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's age drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-age-composition", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("id-age-composition", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("id-age-composition", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection])

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-age-composition (demographic-composition archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Institutional Drift Direction Calculated string DR-159
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's institutional drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-institutional-unit", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("id-institutional-unit", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("id-institutional-unit", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection])

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-institutional-unit (organizational-unit archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Socioeconomic Drift Direction Calculated string DR-160
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's socioeconomic drift direction is computed as the value of LOOKUP("id-socioeconomic-status", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

In plain words

LOOKUP("id-socioeconomic-status", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP("id-socioeconomic-status", ConfounderDistortionTimeline[ConfounderIdentity], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[DriftDirection])

loop-92: ConfounderDistortionTimeline.DriftDirection for id-socioeconomic-status (ses-income archetype), valid-time Type-D drift. Overridden in 02b-customize-functions.sql: native transpiler mistranslates LOOKUP(constant, Table[NonPKColumn], Table[TargetColumn]) into a lookup keyed on the table's PK instead of the specified match column.

ƒ Domain Flip Gap Survives Geometry Control Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-161
In RuleSpeak

A model summary is flagged domain flip gap survives geometry control if all of the following:

    • the economics high imbalance sign flip count is 0
    • the epidemiology high imbalance sign flip rate is greater than 0.15
In plain words

Economics high imbalance sign flip count equals 0 and epidemiology high imbalance sign flip rate is greater than 0.15.

As a formula
= AND(Economics High Imbalance Sign Flip Count = 0, Epidemiology High Imbalance Sign Flip Rate > 0.15)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Economics High Imbalance Sign Flip CountModel Summary Sign-flip count among economics studies with max_stratum_imbalance >= threshold. ●Epidemiology High Imbalance Sign Flip RateModel Summary Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance epidemiology studies.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Economics High Imbalance Sign Flip Count Model Summary ● Epidemiology High Imbalance Sign Flip Rate Model Summary

TRUE when the economics-vs-epidemiology sign-flip-rate gap for H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled survives after restricting to high-imbalance studies (loop-90 native promotion of the former 02b hand function).

ƒ Corpus Pattern Superseded Fail Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-162
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's corpus pattern superseded fail count is computed as the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed plus the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed plus the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed plus the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed plus the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed plus the number of the model summary's discovery findings that are not confirmed.

In plain words

((((count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-econ-zero" and is confirmed is false() plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-dist" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-catalog-flip-prediction" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-econ-encoding-selection" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-profiles-stable" and is confirmed is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-econ-zero", Is Confirmed, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-domain-dist", Is Confirmed, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-catalog-flip-prediction", Is Confirmed, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", Is Confirmed, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-econ-encoding-selection", Is Confirmed, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(Hypothesis ID, "H-domain-profiles-stable", Is Confirmed, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Hypothesis IDDiscovery Finding FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId. ƒIs ConfirmedDiscovery Finding IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-zero", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-dist", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion]), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-latent", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]) >= 5), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-confounder", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-exact-match", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]) > 0.15), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-cplus-magnitude", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-encoding-selection", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-profiles-stable", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate]) < 0.05), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]) < 0.0001, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount]) = 0), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-bidirectional", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-theorem-portfolio", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount]) >= 4, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.40, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-identity-map-coverage", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate]) >= 0.95, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.45, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-mechanism-other-drain", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) <= 5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate]) <= 0.15, LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 5), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction]) >= 0.25, LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-confounder-drift-type-d", AND(OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[InstitutionalDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SocioeconomicDriftDirection]) <> "rising"), FALSE())))))))))))))))))))))))))))))).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding

Count of DiscoveryFindings rows for the 6 superseded-pattern hypotheses (H-econ-zero, H-domain-dist, H-catalog-flip-prediction, H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled, H-econ-encoding-selection, H-domain-profiles-stable) where IsConfirmed=FALSE. Six separate COUNTIFS summed with '+' because this rulebook's formula vocabulary has no IN-list/pipe-delimited OR criterion for COUNTIFS (loop-90 native promotion of the former 02b hand function).

ƒ Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-163
In RuleSpeak

A model summary's expansion wave3 discovery note is computed as “superseded=”, followed by the corpus pattern superseded fail count, followed by “; econFlips=”, followed by the economics sign flip count, followed by “; flipPred=”, followed by the sign flip prediction match rate, followed by “; catalogExact=”, followed by the type prediction match rate, followed by “; theorems=”, followed by the theorem count.

In plain words

"superseded=", corpus pattern superseded fail count, "; econFlips=", economics sign flip count, "; flipPred=", sign flip prediction match rate, "; catalogExact=", type prediction match rate, "; theorems=", joined with theorem count.

As a formula
= CONCAT("superseded=", Corpus Pattern Superseded Fail Count, "; econFlips=", Economics Sign Flip Count, "; flipPred=", Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate, "; catalogExact=", Type Prediction Match Rate, "; theorems=", Theorem Count)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒCorpus Pattern Superseded Fail CountModel Summary ((((count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-econ-zero" and is confirmed is false() plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-dist" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-catalog-flip-prediction" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-econ-encoding-selection" and is confirmed is false()) plus count of discovery findings whose hypothesis ID is "H-domain-profiles-stable" and is confirmed is false(). ƒEconomics Sign Flip CountModel Summary Count of treatment rankings whose study domain is "economics" and is sign flip is true(). ↗Sign Flip Prediction Match RateModel Summary LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]). ↗Type Prediction Match RateModel Summary LOOKUP("catalog-v1", CorpusCatalogSummary[CatalogSummaryId], CorpusCatalogSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]). ƒTheorem CountModel Summary Count of conclusions whose category is "corpus-theorem" and status is "witnessed".
Ground truth at the leaves 14 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Category Conclusion ● Status Conclusion

Expansion-wave-3 supersession-audit synthesis rollup (loop-78, promoted to native loop-90).

Stratum Variable

A stratum variable is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 5 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Confounders in Study Treatment Ranking Σ Confirmed Causal Role Count Treatment Ranking Σ Mediator Risk Count Treatment Ranking Σ Contested Stratum Count Treatment Ranking Σ Unknown Causal Role Count Treatment Ranking Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Causal Claim Status Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary

FK to the parent Study.

● Variable Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The name of the stratifying variable (e.g. stone_size, department, age_group).

● Causal Role Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 4 direct +9 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Confounder Stratum Variable Σ Confirmed Causal Role Count Treatment Ranking Σ Contested Stratum Count Treatment Ranking Σ Unknown Causal Role Count Treatment Ranking Confounders in Study Treatment Ranking Causal Claim Status Treatment Ranking Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary

The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.

● Affects Treatment Assignment Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Confounder Stratum Variable Confounders in Study Treatment Ranking Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary

TRUE if this variable causally influences which treatment a patient receives. Required for confounding.

● Affects Outcome Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Confounder Stratum Variable Confounders in Study Treatment Ranking Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary

TRUE if this variable causally influences the outcome independently of treatment. Required for confounding.

ƒ Is Confounder Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-164
In RuleSpeak

A stratum variable is considered a confounder if all of the following:

    • the affects treatment assignment flag is set
    • the affects outcome flag is set
    • the causal role is “confounder”
In plain words

Affects treatment assignment, affects outcome, and causal role is "confounder".

As a formula
= AND(Affects Treatment Assignment, Affects Outcome, Causal Role = "confounder")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Affects Treatment AssignmentStratum Variable TRUE if this variable causally influences which treatment a patient receives. Required for confounding. ●Affects OutcomeStratum Variable TRUE if this variable causally influences the outcome independently of treatment. Required for confounding. ●Causal RoleStratum Variable The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
Consumers 1 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Confounders in Study Treatment Ranking Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

TRUE when AffectsTreatmentAssignment AND AffectsOutcome AND CausalRole = 'confounder'. A confounder is the classic driver of Simpson's Paradox.

● Mechanism Note Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-language description of how this variable creates the confounding: why it affects both assignment and outcome.

● Conditioning Risk Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Mediator Risk Count Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary

The causal hazard posed by conditioning on this variable. Values: confounder (safe to condition — removes bias), mediator (conditioning blocks the causal path of interest — harmful), collider (conditioning opens a spurious backdoor — harmful), proxy (conditioning on a noisy proxy of the true confounder — risky), none (no conditioning risk identified). NULL means unclassified.

↗ Confounder Identity Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-165
In RuleSpeak

A stratum variable's confounder identity is the value of LOOKUP(StratumVariableId, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StratumVariableId}}, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Stratum Variable ID, StratumVariableIdentityMaps[StratumVariable], StratumVariableIdentityMaps[ConfounderIdentity])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Stratum Variable IDStratum Variable Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-stone-size).
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Stratum Variable ID Stratum Variable

Canonical cross-study identity via StratumVariableIdentityMaps (loop-80).

A treatment ranking is identified by its name and is related to a study.

⇢ Study Relationship string

A pointer to Study. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

● Treatment a Ground truth string

In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's total cases a is the total cases across the case cells related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Cases, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)

Total successes for TreatmentA across all strata.

ƒ Pooled Rate a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-168
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's pooled rate a is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases a is 0
  2. the total successes a divided by the total cases a in all other cases
In plain words

If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases a = 0, "", Total Successes a / Total Cases a)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Cases aTreatment Ranking Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ΣTotal Successes aTreatment Ranking Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

ƒ Pooled Rate B Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-171
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's pooled rate b is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the total cases b is 0
  2. the total successes b divided by the total cases b in all other cases
In plain words

If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases B = 0, "", Total Successes B / Total Cases B)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Cases BTreatment Ranking Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b. ΣTotal Successes BTreatment Ranking Sum of successes across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b.

Pooled success rate for TreatmentB: TotalSuccessesB / TotalCasesB.

ƒ Pooled Winner Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-172
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's pooled winner is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the pooled rate a is blank
    • the pooled rate b is blank
  2. “tie” if the absolute value of the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b is less than 0.0000001
  3. the treatment a if the pooled rate a is greater than the pooled rate b
  4. the treatment b in all other cases
In plain words

If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b.

As a formula
= IF(OR(Pooled Rate a = "", Pooled Rate B = ""), "", IF(ABS(Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B) < 0.0000001, "tie", IF(Pooled Rate a > Pooled Rate B, Treatment a, Treatment B)))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled Rate aTreatment Ranking If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatment Ranking If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatment Ranking Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Strata Won by Loser Treatment Ranking ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement Treatment Ranking Paradox Strength Treatment Ranking Reversal Intensity Treatment Ranking Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Threshold Margin Treatment Ranking Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Which treatment wins when strata are ignored. Returns tie when pooled rates are equal within floating-point tolerance.

Σ Stratum Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-173
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's stratum count is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Consumers 5 direct +39 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Per Stratum Winner Treatment Ranking ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous Treatment Ranking ƒ Paradox Strength Treatment Ranking ƒ Reversal Intensity Treatment Ranking ƒ Threshold Margin Treatment Ranking Unanimous Sign Flip Count Model Summary Distortion Type Treatment Ranking Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Type a Count Model Summary Type B Count Model Summary Type D Count Model Summary C Amplification Count Model Summary C Compression Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary C Plus Avg Distortion Model Summary C Minus Avg Distortion Model Summary Type D Avg Distortion Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Education Type D Count Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Type D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Corrected Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Screening Tier Treatment Ranking Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Distortion Only Count Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Latent Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking

Number of strata in this study. Counted from StratumSummaries filtered to TreatmentA rows (one row per stratum).

Σ Strata Won by a Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-174
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's strata won by a is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a, Stratum Winner, Treatment a)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ƒStratum WinnerStratum Summary If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B".

Number of strata where A wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentA. These are the one-per-stratum sentinel rows.

Σ Strata Won by B Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-175
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's strata won by b is the number of stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a, Stratum Winner, Treatment B)
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ƒStratum WinnerStratum Summary If stratum rate a is greater than stratum rate b then "A", otherwise "B". ●Treatment BTreatment Ranking Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
Consumers 3 direct +42 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Per Stratum Winner Treatment Ranking ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous Treatment Ranking ƒ Strata Won by Loser Treatment Ranking Unanimous Sign Flip Count Model Summary Distortion Type Treatment Ranking Paradox Strength Treatment Ranking Reversal Intensity Treatment Ranking Type a Count Model Summary Type B Count Model Summary Type D Count Model Summary C Amplification Count Model Summary C Compression Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary C Plus Avg Distortion Model Summary C Minus Avg Distortion Model Summary Type D Avg Distortion Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Education Type D Count Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Type D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Corrected Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Screening Tier Treatment Ranking Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Threshold Margin Treatment Ranking Type a Fraction Model Summary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Distortion Only Count Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Latent Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary

⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Number of strata where B wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentB.

ƒ Per Stratum Winner Calculated 5 hops from ground truth string DR-176
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's per stratum winner is determined by the following priority:

  1. the treatment a if the strata won by a is the stratum count
  2. the treatment b if the strata won by b is the stratum count
  3. “none” in all other cases
In plain words

If strata won by a equals stratum count then treatment a, otherwise if strata won by b equals stratum count then treatment b, otherwise "none".

As a formula
= IF(Strata Won by a = Stratum Count, Treatment a, IF(Strata Won by B = Stratum Count, Treatment B, "none"))
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ΣStrata Won by aTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a. ΣStratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ΣStrata Won by BTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b. ●Treatment BTreatment Ranking Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

The treatment that wins in every stratum — 'A' if StrataWonByA = StratumCount, 'B' if StrataWonByB = StratumCount, else 'none' (no unanimous per-stratum winner).

ƒ Is Stratum Unanimous Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-177
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking is considered stratum-unanimous if all of the following:

    • the strata won by a is the stratum count
    • the strata won by b is the stratum count
In plain words

Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count.

As a formula
= OR(Strata Won by a = Stratum Count, Strata Won by B = Stratum Count)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣStrata Won by aTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a. ΣStratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ΣStrata Won by BTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b.
Consumers 2 direct +35 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Unanimous Sign Flip Count Model Summary ƒ Distortion Type Treatment Ranking Type a Count Model Summary Type B Count Model Summary Type D Count Model Summary C Amplification Count Model Summary C Compression Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary C Plus Avg Distortion Model Summary C Minus Avg Distortion Model Summary Type D Avg Distortion Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Education Type D Count Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Type D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Corrected Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Screening Tier Treatment Ranking Type a Fraction Model Summary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Distortion Only Count Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Latent Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
