⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when one treatment wins every stratum (StrataWonByA = StratumCount OR StrataWonByB = StratumCount). Maps A/B policy split: unanimous → stratify-immediately geometry; heterogeneous → investigate-confounder.

ƒ Is Reversal Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-178
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's is reversal is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the is sign flip is blank
  2. the is sign flip in all other cases
In plain words

If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip.

As a formula
= IF(Is Sign Flip = "", "", Is Sign Flip)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Reversal Count Model Summary Σ Non Reversal Count Model Summary ƒ Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Study Count Model Summary Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when the equal-weight pooled signal (WeightedStratumGapSum) and the actual pooled signal (SignedPooledGap) point in opposite directions — allocation has flipped the direction of the aggregate conclusion. This is Simpson's Paradox as a derived fact.

Σ Confounders in Study Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-179
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's confounders in study is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that are confounders.

In plain words

Count of stratum variables whose study is study and is confounder is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Is Confounder, TRUE())
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Variable FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ƒIs ConfounderStratum Variable Affects treatment assignment, affects outcome, and causal role is "confounder".
Consumers 1 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Paradox Explained Treatment Ranking Explained Count Model Summary Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Count of StratumVariables in this study whose IsConfounder = TRUE. When > 0, the paradox has a causal explanation.

ƒ Is Paradox Explained Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-180
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking is considered paradox-explained if all of the following:

    • the reversal flag is set
    • the confounders in study is greater than 0
In plain words

Is reversal and confounders in study is greater than 0.

As a formula
= AND(Is Reversal, Confounders in Study > 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ReversalTreatment Ranking If is sign flip is "" then "", otherwise is sign flip. ΣConfounders in StudyTreatment Ranking Count of stratum variables whose study is study and is confounder is true().
Consumers 5 direct rules that read this one
Σ Explained Count Model Summary Σ Explained Confounder Count Model Summary Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary Σ False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Σ Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 15 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Variable ● Affects Treatment Assignment Stratum Variable ● Affects Outcome Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

TRUE when IsReversal is present AND at least one confirmed confounder exists in the study.

ƒ Pooled Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-181
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the pooled rate a is blank
  2. the absolute value of the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b in all other cases
In plain words

If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).

As a formula
= IF(Pooled Rate a = "", "", ABS(Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled Rate aTreatment Ranking If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatment Ranking If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).
Consumers 4 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Σ Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary ƒ Paradox Strength Treatment Ranking ƒ Allocation Fragility Treatment Ranking Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Absolute difference between the two pooled rates: |PooledRateA - PooledRateB|. The size of the aggregate misleading signal.

ƒ Strata Won by Loser Calculated 5 hops from ground truth integer DR-182
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's strata won by loser is determined by the following priority:

  1. the strata won by b if the pooled winner is the treatment a
  2. the strata won by a in all other cases
In plain words

If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.

As a formula
= IF(Pooled Winner = Treatment a, Strata Won by B, Strata Won by a)
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled WinnerTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b. ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ΣStrata Won by BTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment b. ΣStrata Won by aTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study, treatment label is treatment a, and stratum winner is treatment a.
Consumers 2 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Paradox Strength Treatment Ranking ƒ Reversal Intensity Treatment Ranking Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Threshold Margin Treatment Ranking Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Number of strata won by the pooled loser — the counter-signal. For full reversals this equals StratumCount; for partial paradoxes it is between 0 and StratumCount.

ƒ Paradox Strength Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-183
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's paradox strength is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
  2. the pooled gap times the strata won by loser divided by the stratum count in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (pooled gap times (strata won by loser divided by stratum count)).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Pooled Gap * (Strata Won by Loser / Stratum Count))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣStratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒPooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒStrata Won by LoserTreatment Ranking If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Total Paradox Strength Model Summary Avg Paradox Strength Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

Scalar severity of the paradox: PooledGap × (StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount). Zero when no strata go against the pooled winner. Positive for partial paradoxes. Maximum when every stratum contradicts the pooled result.

Σ Pooled Rate From Weights a Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-184
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's pooled rate from weights a is the total weighted stratum rate across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Sum of weighted stratum rate across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Rate, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒWeighted Stratum RateStratum Summary If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate). ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking

Pooled rate for TreatmentA reconstructed as a weighted average of stratum rates: SUM(WeightedStratumRate) across all stratum rows for TreatmentA. Must match PooledRateA — this witnesses the mechanism equation: the pooled rate IS a weighted average of stratum rates, weighted by StratumFraction.

Σ Pooled Rate From Weights B Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth number DR-185
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's pooled rate from weights b is the total weighted stratum rate across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Sum of weighted stratum rate across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Rate, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment B)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒWeighted Stratum RateStratum Summary If stratum fraction is "" then "", otherwise (stratum fraction times stratum success rate). ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatment Ranking Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Pooled rate for TreatmentB reconstructed as a weighted average. Must match PooledRateB. If it does, the mechanism equation is verified: reversal is purely a consequence of differential weighting.

ƒ Reversal Intensity Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-186
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's reversal intensity is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
  2. the strata won by loser divided by the stratum count in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (strata won by loser divided by stratum count).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Strata Won by Loser / Stratum Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣStratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒStrata Won by LoserTreatment Ranking If pooled winner equals treatment a then strata won by b, otherwise strata won by a.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Threshold Margin Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount: the fraction of strata that go against the pooled winner. Zero for no paradox, 1.0 for full reversal, between 0 and 1 for partial. This is the allocation-side measure of how deeply the confounding has penetrated the stratified view.

ƒ Threshold Margin Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-187
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's threshold margin is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the stratum count is 0
  2. the reversal intensity minus 0.5 in all other cases
In plain words

If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (reversal intensity minus 0.5).

As a formula
= IF(Stratum Count = 0, "", Reversal Intensity - 0.5)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣStratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒReversal IntensityTreatment Ranking If stratum count equals 0 then "", otherwise (strata won by loser divided by stratum count).
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary

ReversalIntensity minus 0.5: positive when more than half the strata go against the pooled winner (reversal is robust), zero at the tipping point (exactly half), negative when fewer than half oppose the pooled winner (reversal is fragile or absent). A study with ThresholdMargin > 0 is robustly paradoxical; one with ThresholdMargin < 0 has a weak or absent paradox.

ƒ Signed Pooled Gap Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-188
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's signed pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the pooled rate a is blank
  2. the pooled rate a minus the pooled rate b in all other cases
In plain words

If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).

As a formula
= IF(Pooled Rate a = "", "", Pooled Rate a - Pooled Rate B)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled Rate aTreatment Ranking If total cases a equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes a divided by total cases a). ƒPooled Rate BTreatment Ranking If total cases b equals 0 then "", otherwise (total successes b divided by total cases b).
Consumers 5 direct +63 transitive rules that read this one

Signed difference between pooled rates: PooledRateA minus PooledRateB. Positive = A wins pooled; negative = B wins pooled. Unlike PooledGap (which is absolute), this preserves direction — essential for detecting sign flips between the equal-weight and actual pooled signals.

Σ Weighted Stratum Gap Sum Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-189
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's weighted stratum gap sum is the total weighted stratum gap across the stratum summaries related to the treatment ranking.

In plain words

Sum of weighted stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.

As a formula
= SUMIFS(Weighted Stratum Gap, Study, Study, Treatment Label, Treatment a)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒWeighted Stratum GapStratum Summary If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise (stratum gap times stratum fraction). ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking

Sum of WeightedStratumGap (= StratumGap × StratumFraction) across all StratumSummaries rows where TreatmentLabel = TreatmentA for this study. This is the equal-weight pooled gap — the gap the pooled analysis would produce if allocation were perfectly balanced across strata. When this has the opposite sign to SignedPooledGap, the allocation has not merely compressed the signal: it has flipped it entirely.

ƒ Is Sign Flip Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-190
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's is sign flip is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the weighted stratum gap sum is blank
  2. the signed pooled gap is less than 0 if the weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0
  3. the signed pooled gap is greater than 0 in all other cases
In plain words

If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.

As a formula
= IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum = "", "", IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum > 0, Signed Pooled Gap < 0, Signed Pooled Gap > 0))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatment Ranking Sum of weighted stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).

● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when WeightedStratumGapSum and SignedPooledGap have opposite signs. Same condition as IsReversal; retained as the coordinate-level name in the DAG.

ƒ Allocation Distortion Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-191
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's allocation distortion is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the weighted stratum gap sum is blank
  2. the absolute value of the weighted stratum gap sum minus the signed pooled gap in all other cases
In plain words

If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap).

As a formula
= IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum = "", "", ABS(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum - Signed Pooled Gap))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatment Ranking Sum of weighted stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
Consumers 8 direct +36 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Epidemiology Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ Education Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ C Plus Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ C Minus Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ Type D Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ Distortion Type Treatment Ranking ƒ Signal Purity Treatment Ranking ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Type a Count Model Summary Type B Count Model Summary Type D Count Model Summary C Amplification Count Model Summary C Compression Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Education Type D Count Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Type D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Corrected Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking Screening Tier Treatment Ranking Avg Signal Purity Model Summary Avg Signal Purity Reversal Model Summary Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Model Summary Sign Flip Signal Purity Max Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Distortion Only Count Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Latent Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Signal Purity Gap Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

The magnitude of how far the allocation has bent the pooled signal: |WeightedStratumGapSum − SignedPooledGap|. Zero means the allocation is neutral — the pooled analysis faithfully represents the equal-weight stratum evidence. A large AllocationDistortion means the allocation is doing most of the work: the pooled number is mostly noise from how cases were assigned, not signal about which treatment is better. This measure does NOT require a sign flip to be nonzero — it captures any allocation-induced distortion, not just reversals.

ƒ Distortion Type Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-192
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's distortion type is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the allocation distortion is blank
  2. “A” if the stratum unanimous flag is set, in all other cases “B” if the sign flip flag is set
  3. “C+” if all of these hold:
    • the sign flip flag is not set
    • the allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
    • the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the corrected gap plus 0.001
  4. “C-” if all of these hold:
    • the sign flip flag is not set
    • the allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
    • the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the corrected gap minus 0.001
  5. “D” in all other cases
In plain words

If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".

As a formula
= IF(Allocation Distortion = "", "", IF(Is Sign Flip, IF(Is Stratum Unanimous, "A", "B"), IF(AND(NOT(Is Sign Flip), Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Corrected Gap) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT(Is Sign Flip), Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Corrected Gap) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒIs Stratum UnanimousTreatment Ranking Strata won by a equals stratum count or strata won by b equals stratum count. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b). ƒCorrected GapTreatment Ranking Weighted stratum gap sum.
Consumers 21 direct +14 transitive rules that read this one
