● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Geometric classification (five types A/B/C+/C-/D). Sign-flip types: A = unanimous stratum direction (IsStratumUnanimous); B = heterogeneous stratum directions. Non-flip: C+/C-/D by distortion ratio.

ƒ Policy Implication Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-193
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's policy implication is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
  2. “stratify-immediately” if the distortion type is “A”
  3. “investigate-confounder” if the distortion type is “B”
  4. “check-allocation-bias” if any of these hold:
    • the distortion type is “C+”
    • the distortion type is “C-”
  5. “pooled-analysis-trustworthy” in all other cases
In plain words

If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise if distortion type is "A" then "stratify-immediately", otherwise if distortion type is "B" then "investigate-confounder", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "check-allocation-bias", otherwise "pooled-analysis-trustworthy".

As a formula
= IF(Distortion Type = "", "", IF(Distortion Type = "A", "stratify-immediately", IF(Distortion Type = "B", "investigate-confounder", IF(OR(Distortion Type = "C+", Distortion Type = "C-"), "check-allocation-bias", "pooled-analysis-trustworthy"))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

The researcher action implied by the geometric classification. Derived from DistortionType: type A (full reversal) → 'stratify-immediately' — the pooled conclusion is directionally wrong; do not act on it without stratifying. Type B (partial sign-flip) → 'investigate-confounder' — the equal-weight signal opposes the pooled signal, but the per-stratum picture is mixed; the causal mechanism needs examination before trusting either estimate. Type C (compression without flip) → 'check-allocation-bias' — the pooled winner is probably correct but the effect size is distorted; report adjusted or standardized margins rather than the raw pooled gap. Type D (neutral) → 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy' — allocation is not materially distorting the signal; the pooled conclusion is safe with respect to this stratification.

ƒ Corrected Gap Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-194
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's corrected gap is the same as its weighted stratum gap sum.

In plain words

Weighted stratum gap sum.

As a formula
= Weighted Stratum Gap Sum
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatment Ranking Sum of weighted stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a.
Consumers 4 direct +41 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Distortion Type Treatment Ranking ƒ Corrected Winner Treatment Ranking ƒ Allocation Direction Treatment Ranking ƒ Signal Purity Treatment Ranking Type a Count Model Summary Type B Count Model Summary Type D Count Model Summary C Amplification Count Model Summary C Compression Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary C Plus Avg Distortion Model Summary C Minus Avg Distortion Model Summary Type D Avg Distortion Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Education Type D Count Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Type D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Corrected Policy Implication Treatment Ranking Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Screening Tier Treatment Ranking Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement Treatment Ranking Avg Signal Purity Reversal Model Summary Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Model Summary Avg Signal Purity Model Summary Sign Flip Signal Purity Max Model Summary Type a Fraction Model Summary Distortion Taxonomy Coverage Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Distortion Only Count Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Latent Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary Danger Tier Count Model Summary Caution Tier Count Model Summary Safe Tier Count Model Summary Signal Purity Gap Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 10 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking

The allocation-corrected treatment gap: WeightedStratumGapSum, i.e. what SignedPooledGap would be if allocation were equal across strata. Positive means A is favoured in the corrected world; negative means B is favoured.

ƒ Corrected Winner Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-195
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's corrected winner is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
  2. “tie” if the absolute value of the corrected gap is less than 0.0001
  3. the treatment a if the corrected gap is greater than 0
  4. the treatment b if the corrected gap is less than 0
  5. “tie” in all other cases
In plain words

If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of corrected gap is less than 0.0001 then "tie", otherwise if corrected gap is greater than 0 then treatment a, otherwise if corrected gap is less than 0 then treatment b, otherwise "tie".

As a formula
= IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(ABS(Corrected Gap) < 0.0001, "tie", IF(Corrected Gap > 0, Treatment a, IF(Corrected Gap < 0, Treatment B, "tie"))))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒCorrected GapTreatment Ranking Weighted stratum gap sum. ●Treatment aTreatment Ranking Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel). ●Treatment BTreatment Ranking Label for the second treatment in the comparison.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Which treatment wins once allocation bias is removed. Derived from CorrectedGap: positive → TreatmentA; negative → TreatmentB; near-zero → tie.

ƒ Corrected Vs Pooled Agreement Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-196
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's corrected vs pooled agreement is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the corrected winner is blank
  2. the corrected winner is the pooled winner in all other cases
In plain words

If corrected winner is "" then "", otherwise corrected winner equals pooled winner.

As a formula
= IF(Corrected Winner = "", "", Corrected Winner = Pooled Winner)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒCorrected WinnerTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of corrected gap is less than 0.0001 then "tie", otherwise if corrected gap is greater than 0 then treatment a, otherwise if corrected gap is less than 0 then treatment b, otherwise "tie". ƒPooled WinnerTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" or pooled rate b is "" then "", otherwise if the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b) is less than 0.0000001 then "tie", otherwise if pooled rate a is greater than pooled rate b then treatment a, otherwise treatment b.
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when the allocation-corrected winner matches the pooled winner. FALSE when removing allocation bias would change which treatment appears to win — the machine-readable definition of a reversal recovery.

ƒ Corrected Policy Implication Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-197
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's corrected policy implication is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
  2. “use-corrected-winner” if the distortion type is “A”
  3. “use-corrected-winner-with-caution” if the distortion type is “B”
  4. “check-allocation-bias” if any of these hold:
    • the distortion type is “C+”
    • the distortion type is “C-”
  5. “pooled-analysis-trustworthy” in all other cases
In plain words

If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise if distortion type is "A" then "use-corrected-winner", otherwise if distortion type is "B" then "use-corrected-winner-with-caution", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "check-allocation-bias", otherwise "pooled-analysis-trustworthy".

As a formula
= IF(Distortion Type = "", "", IF(Distortion Type = "A", "use-corrected-winner", IF(Distortion Type = "B", "use-corrected-winner-with-caution", IF(OR(Distortion Type = "C+", Distortion Type = "C-"), "check-allocation-bias", "pooled-analysis-trustworthy"))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

The allocation-aware researcher action: what to do when the corrected verdict is available. Derived from DistortionType and CorrectedWinner together, making the instrument self-consistent with Reversal Recovery (loop-27). Type A (full sign-flip, unanimous per-stratum reversal): CorrectedWinner is the true signal — 'use-corrected-winner'. The pooled analysis was directionally wrong; act on CorrectedWinner, not PooledWinner. Type B (partial sign-flip, non-unanimous): CorrectedWinner points against the pooled signal but strata disagree among themselves — 'use-corrected-winner-with-caution'. Trust the corrected direction but acknowledge residual uncertainty. Type C (compression, no sign flip): PooledWinner is directionally correct; the distortion is in the magnitude, not the direction — 'check-allocation-bias'. Same as PolicyImplication. Type D (neutral): allocation is not materially distorting the signal — 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy'. Same as PolicyImplication. For Types C and D, CorrectedPolicyImplication and PolicyImplication always agree; for Types A and B, CorrectedPolicyImplication supersedes PolicyImplication with the allocation-corrected verdict.

ƒ Allocation Direction Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-198
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's allocation direction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
  2. “reversal” if the sign flip flag is set
  3. “amplification” if the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the corrected gap plus 0.001
  4. “compression” if the absolute value of the signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the corrected gap minus 0.001
  5. “neutral” in all other cases
In plain words

If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then "reversal", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "amplification", otherwise if the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "compression", otherwise "neutral".

As a formula
= IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(Is Sign Flip, "reversal", IF(ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Corrected Gap) + 0.001, "amplification", IF(ABS(Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Corrected Gap) - 0.001, "compression", "neutral"))))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒCorrected GapTreatment Ranking Weighted stratum gap sum. ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Avg Signal Purity Reversal Model Summary ƒ Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Model Summary Signal Purity Gap Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Whether the allocation is reversing, amplifying, or compressing the true treatment signal (CorrectedGap). 'reversal': allocation changed the sign of the pooled gap relative to CorrectedGap — worst case; pooled winner is wrong (types A and B). 'amplification': allocation inflated the pooled gap beyond CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is overstated (type C+). 'compression': allocation shrank the pooled gap below CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is understated (type C-). 'neutral': allocation had negligible effect on the pooled gap (type D). Policy consequences: reversal → wrong decision; amplification → overconfidence; compression → underconfidence / premature abandonment; neutral → trustworthy.

ƒ Signal Purity Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-199
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's signal purity is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the corrected gap is blank
  2. 1 if the absolute value of the corrected gap plus the allocation distortion is 0
  3. the absolute value of the corrected gap divided by the absolute value of the corrected gap plus the allocation distortion in all other cases
In plain words

If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)).

As a formula
= IF(Corrected Gap = "", "", IF(ABS(Corrected Gap) + Allocation Distortion = 0, 1, ABS(Corrected Gap) / (ABS(Corrected Gap) + Allocation Distortion)))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒCorrected GapTreatment Ranking Weighted stratum gap sum. ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap).
Consumers 5 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Avg Signal Purity Model Summary ƒ Avg Signal Purity Reversal Model Summary ƒ Avg Signal Purity Non Reversal Model Summary ƒ Sign Flip Signal Purity Max Model Summary ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Signal Purity Gap Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

The fraction of total observed variation (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion) that is genuine treatment signal rather than allocation noise. SignalPurity = |CorrectedGap| / (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion). Range [0,1]: 1.0 = allocation contributed nothing (type D); 0 = corrected gap is zero (pure noise). Critical threshold is 0.5: SignalPurity < 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal. The signal-purity theorem (loop-44): AllocationDirection='reversal' → SignalPurity < 0.5. Equivalently: a sign flip requires the confound to produce more distortion than the treatment produced signal. This is the mechanistic account of why reversals happen: not study-design accidents but algebraic necessities.

ƒ Pooled Gap Crosses Zero Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-200
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking is flagged pooled gap crosses zero if the value of LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study.
Consumers 3 direct +15 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Cross Zero Count Model Summary ƒ Latent Flip Potential Treatment Ranking ƒ Is Latent Only Flip Treatment Ranking Latent Type D Count Model Summary Stable Type D Count Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Education Latent D Count Model Summary Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Confounder Latent Only Count Model Summary Collider Selection Latent Only Count Model Summary Education Latent Fraction Model Summary Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Lookup: SweepStudySummary.PooledGapCrossesZero via Study. TRUE when reweighting allocation would change the sign of the pooled gap.

ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-201
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's sweep pooled gap range is computed as the value of LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study.
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Allocation Fragility Treatment Ranking ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Lookup: SweepStudySummary.SweepPooledGapRange via Study. Width of pooled-gap wander under allocation sweep.

ƒ Latent Flip Potential Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-202
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's latent flip potential is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the distortion type is blank
  2. the AND of the distortion type is “D” and the pooled gap crosses zero is true in all other cases
In plain words

If distortion type is "" then "", otherwise distortion type is "D" and pooled gap crosses zero equals yes.

As a formula
= IF(Distortion Type = "", "", AND(Distortion Type = "D", Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = TRUE))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]).
Consumers 6 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Latent Type D Count Model Summary ƒ Stable Type D Count Model Summary Σ Sum Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Σ Sum Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary ƒ Education Latent D Count Model Summary ƒ Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Latent Type D Fraction Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Latent D Model Summary Avg Pooled Gap Stable D Model Summary Discovery Witness Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when DistortionType=D but PooledGapCrossesZero=TRUE — SAFE at observed allocation but flip-capable under counterfactual reweighting.

ƒ Allocation Fragility Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-203
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's allocation fragility is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if any of these hold:
    • the sweep pooled gap range is blank
    • the pooled gap is blank
    • the pooled gap is 0
  2. the sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of the pooled gap in all other cases
In plain words

If sweep pooled gap range is "", pooled gap is "", or pooled gap equals 0 then 0, otherwise (sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of pooled gap).

As a formula
= IF(OR(Sweep Pooled Gap Range = "", Pooled Gap = "", Pooled Gap = 0), 0, Sweep Pooled Gap Range / ABS(Pooled Gap))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Pooled Gap RangeTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]). ƒPooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Treatment Ranking Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

SweepPooledGapRange divided by |PooledGap|. High values mean small observed effect but wide counterfactual wander. Fixed loop-90: this rulebook's IF(x="","",...) blank-on-degenerate idiom transpiles to a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime the moment a live row genuinely has PooledGap=0 (surfaced only once _erb_tr_metrics, which masked this row, was eliminated). Returns 0 for the degenerate case instead — see TreatmentRankings.DistortionRatio for the same fix pattern.

Σ Max Stratum Imbalance Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-204
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's max stratum imbalance is the largest abs allocation bias across the treatment ranking's stratum summaries that have a treatment label of “A”.

In plain words

Largest abs allocation bias across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is "A".

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Abs Allocation Bias, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "A")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒAbs Allocation BiasStratum Summary If allocation bias is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of allocation bias. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

MAXIFS(StratumSummaries.AbsAllocationBias) over rows where Study matches and TreatmentLabel='A' — the largest allocation-share skew any single stratum shows for this study. Live aggregation; replaces the former hand-written _erb_tr_metrics cache column of the same name.

Σ Max Stratum Gap Aggregation 5 hops from ground truth number DR-205
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's max stratum gap is the largest abs stratum gap across the treatment ranking's stratum summaries that have a treatment label of “A”.

In plain words

Largest abs stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is "A".

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Abs Stratum Gap, Study, Study, Treatment Label, "A")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒAbs Stratum GapStratum Summary If stratum gap is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of stratum gap. ⇢StudyStratum Summary FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Treatment LabelStratum Summary Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).
Ground truth at the leaves 9 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary

MAXIFS(StratumSummaries.AbsStratumGap) over rows where Study matches and TreatmentLabel='A' — the largest within-stratum rate gap for this study. Live aggregation; replaces the former hand-written _erb_tr_metrics cache column of the same name.

ƒ Is Sweep Fragile Calculated 8 hops from ground truth boolean DR-206
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking is considered a sweep fragile if all of the following:

    • the distortion type is “D”
    • the signal purity is 1
    • the allocation distortion is 0
    • the sweep pooled gap range is greater than 0.3
    • the allocation fragility is at least 10
In plain words

Distortion type is "D", signal purity equals 1, allocation distortion equals 0, sweep pooled gap range is greater than 0.3, and allocation fragility is at least 10.

As a formula
= AND(Distortion Type = "D", Signal Purity = 1, Allocation Distortion = 0, Sweep Pooled Gap Range > 0.3, Allocation Fragility >= 10)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D". ƒSignal PurityTreatment Ranking If corrected gap is "" then "", otherwise if (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion) equals 0 then 1, otherwise (the absolute value of corrected gap divided by (the absolute value of corrected gap plus allocation distortion)). ƒAllocation DistortionTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise the absolute value of (weighted stratum gap sum minus signed pooled gap). ƒSweep Pooled Gap RangeTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[SweepPooledGapRange]). ƒAllocation FragilityTreatment Ranking If sweep pooled gap range is "", pooled gap is "", or pooled gap equals 0 then 0, otherwise (sweep pooled gap range divided by the absolute value of pooled gap).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Fragile Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

Type-D study that is pooled-safe but ultra-fragile under allocation reweighting: signal_purity=1, allocation_distortion=0, sweep_pooled_gap_range>0.3, allocation_fragility>=10.

ƒ Study Domain Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-207
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's study domain is computed as the value of LOOKUP(Study, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{Study}}, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study, Studies[StudyId], Studies[Domain])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study.
Consumers 10 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Economics Sign Flip Count Model Summary ƒ Epidemiology Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ Education Avg Distortion Model Summary ƒ Education Latent Fraction Model Summary ƒ Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary ƒ Economics Sign Flip Rate Model Summary ƒ Education Type D Count Model Summary ƒ Education Latent D Count Model Summary ƒ Sports Type D Count Model Summary ƒ Sports Latent D Count Model Summary Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Lookup: Studies.Domain via Study.

↗ Stratum Causal Role Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-208
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's stratum causal role is the value of LOOKUP(Study, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{Study}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[CausalRole])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study.
Consumers 7 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary ƒ Confounder Latent Only Count Model Summary ƒ Collider Selection Count Model Summary ƒ Collider Selection Manifest Count Model Summary ƒ Collider Selection Latent Only Count Model Summary Σ False Positive Explained Count Model Summary Σ Unexplained Confounder Sign Flip Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking

Lookup: StratumVariables.CausalRole for this study — confounder, mediator, collider, selection, contested, etc.

ƒ Is Latent Only Flip Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-209
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's is latent only flip is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the pooled gap crosses zero is blank
  2. the AND of the pooled gap crosses zero is true and the is sign flip is false in all other cases
In plain words

If pooled gap crosses zero is "" then "", otherwise pooled gap crosses zero equals true() and is sign flip equals false().

As a formula
= IF(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = "", "", AND(Pooled Gap Crosses Zero = TRUE(), Is Sign Flip = FALSE()))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPooled Gap Crosses ZeroTreatment Ranking LOOKUP({{Study}}, SweepStudySummary[SweepStudyId], SweepStudySummary[PooledGapCrossesZero]). ƒIs Sign FlipTreatment Ranking If weighted stratum gap sum is "" then "", otherwise if weighted stratum gap sum is greater than 0 then signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise signed pooled gap is greater than 0.
Consumers 4 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Confounder Latent Only Count Model Summary ƒ Collider Selection Latent Only Count Model Summary ƒ Education Latent Fraction Model Summary ƒ Sports Latent Fraction Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TRUE when allocation sweep crosses zero but observed allocation shows no sign flip — latent-only reversal.

Σ Confirmed Causal Role Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-210
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's confirmed causal role count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “confounder”.

In plain words

Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "confounder".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "confounder")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Variable FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratum Variable The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Causal Claim Status Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is "confounder" — includes variables that confound the outcome regardless of whether allocation was balanced.

Σ Mediator Risk Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-211
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's mediator risk count is the number of the treatment ranking's mediator risks.

In plain words

Count of stratum variables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "mediator" plus count of stratum variables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "collider".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Conditioning Risk, "mediator") + COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Conditioning Risk, "collider")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Variable FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Conditioning RiskStratum Variable The causal hazard posed by conditioning on this variable. Values: confounder (safe to condition — removes bias), mediator (conditioning blocks the causal path of interest — harmful), collider (conditioning opens a spurious backdoor — harmful), proxy (conditioning on a noisy proxy of the true confounder — risky), none (no conditioning risk identified). NULL means unclassified.
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Conditioning Risk Stratum Variable

Count of StratumVariables in this study whose ConditioningRisk is mediator or collider — the hazardous conditioning types.

Σ Contested Stratum Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-212
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's contested stratum count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “contested”.

In plain words

Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "contested".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "contested")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Variable FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratum Variable The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Causal Claim Status Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is contested — the causal interpretation is disputed.

Σ Unknown Causal Role Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-213
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's unknown causal role count is the number of the treatment ranking's stratum variables that have a causal role of “unknown”.

In plain words

Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "unknown".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Study, Study, Causal Role, "unknown")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
⇢StudyStratum Variable FK to the parent Study. ⇢StudyTreatment Ranking FK to the parent Study. ●Causal RoleStratum Variable The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Causal Claim Status Treatment Ranking Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is unknown — no causal account has been established.

ƒ Causal Claim Status Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-214
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's causal claim status is determined by the following priority:

  1. “contested” if the contested stratum count is greater than 0
  2. “geometric-only” if the unknown causal role count is greater than 0
  3. “established” if the confirmed causal role count is greater than 0
  4. “geometric-only” in all other cases
In plain words

If contested stratum count is greater than 0 then "contested", otherwise if unknown causal role count is greater than 0 then "geometric-only", otherwise if confirmed causal role count is greater than 0 then "established", otherwise "geometric-only".

As a formula
= IF(Contested Stratum Count > 0, "contested", IF(Unknown Causal Role Count > 0, "geometric-only", IF(Confirmed Causal Role Count > 0, "established", "geometric-only")))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ΣContested Stratum CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "contested". ΣUnknown Causal Role CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "unknown". ΣConfirmed Causal Role CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum variables whose study is study and causal role is "confounder".
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Adjustment Appropriate Treatment Ranking Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

The epistemic status of the causal interpretation for this study. Values: established (all stratum variables are confirmed confounders with known mechanisms), contested (at least one stratum variable has a disputed causal role — e.g. Berkeley 1973 where department choice may be endogenous), geometric-only (no stratum variable has an established causal role — the distortion is a geometric fact, not a causal claim). Derived from StratumVariables.CausalRole and ConditioningRisk.

ƒ Adjustment Appropriate Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-215
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking is flagged adjustment appropriate if all of the following:

    • the mediator risk count is 0
    • the causal claim status is “established”
In plain words

Mediator risk count equals 0 and causal claim status is "established".

As a formula
= AND(Mediator Risk Count = 0, Causal Claim Status = "established")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣMediator Risk CountTreatment Ranking Count of stratum variables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "mediator" plus count of stratum variables whose study is study and conditioning risk is "collider". ƒCausal Claim StatusTreatment Ranking If contested stratum count is greater than 0 then "contested", otherwise if unknown causal role count is greater than 0 then "geometric-only", otherwise if confirmed causal role count is greater than 0 then "established", otherwise "geometric-only".
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
Σ Contested or Mediator Explained Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Study Stratum Variable ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Conditioning Risk Stratum Variable ● Causal Role Stratum Variable

TRUE only when it is epistemically safe to act on CorrectedGap as the causal treatment effect estimate: no mediator/collider conditioning risk AND causal claim is established (not contested or geometric-only). FALSE for the birth-weight paradox (mediator), berkeley-1973 (contested), and synthetic/degenerate studies (geometric-only).

● Adjustment Rationale Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-authored note explaining why AdjustmentAppropriate is TRUE or FALSE for this study. Covers mediator/collider hazards, contested causal roles, and cases where geometric correction is valid but causal interpretation is uncertain.

ƒ Purity Trap Flag Calculated boolean DR-216
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's purity trap flag is a defined attribute of the treatment ranking.

In plain words

This roll-up is defined structurally over a relationship — no spreadsheet formula to read aloud. See RuleSpeak.

As a formula

No spreadsheet formula — a structural roll-up. See RuleSpeak.

TRUE when SignalPurity >= 0.90 AND the study's confounder identity has PolicyDefault IN ('do-not-condition','do-not-naively-adjust'). Marks the purity trap: data appears cleanest where naive stratification is most harmful.

ƒ Distortion Ratio Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-217
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's distortion ratio is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the weighted stratum gap sum is 0
  2. the signed pooled gap divided by the weighted stratum gap sum in all other cases
In plain words

If weighted stratum gap sum equals 0 then 0, otherwise (signed pooled gap divided by weighted stratum gap sum).

As a formula
= IF(Weighted Stratum Gap Sum = 0, 0, Signed Pooled Gap / Weighted Stratum Gap Sum)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣWeighted Stratum Gap SumTreatment Ranking Sum of weighted stratum gap across all stratum summaries whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ƒSigned Pooled GapTreatment Ranking If pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (pooled rate a minus pooled rate b).
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

SignedPooledGap / WeightedStratumGapSum. Promoted loop-90 from a 03b-customize-views.sql presentation-layer bolt-on to a first-class rulebook field. The IF(x=0,"",...) blank-on-zero-denominator idiom used elsewhere in this rulebook is unsafe here: NULLIF is not a supported transpiler function, and the transpiler's translation of the "" branch for a numeric-typed field is a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime the moment a live row actually hits it (4 of 242 TreatmentRankings rows have WeightedStratumGapSum=0). Returns 0 in that case instead — WeightedStratumGapSum=0 means there is no equal-weight baseline signal to compare against, so a 0 ratio (no distortion multiplier) is the defensible convention, not a guess.

ƒ Screening Tier Calculated 8 hops from ground truth string DR-218
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's screening tier is determined by the following priority:

  1. “DANGER” if any of these hold:
    • the distortion type is “A”
    • the distortion type is “B”
  2. “CAUTION” if any of these hold:
    • the distortion type is “C+”
    • the distortion type is “C-”
  3. “SAFE” if the distortion type is “D”
  4. an empty string in all other cases
In plain words

If distortion type is "A" or distortion type is "B" then "DANGER", otherwise if distortion type is "C+" or distortion type is "C-" then "CAUTION", otherwise if distortion type is "D" then "SAFE", otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(OR(Distortion Type = "A", Distortion Type = "B"), "DANGER", IF(OR(Distortion Type = "C+", Distortion Type = "C-"), "CAUTION", IF(Distortion Type = "D", "SAFE", "")))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒDistortion TypeTreatment Ranking If allocation distortion is "" then "", otherwise if is sign flip then if is stratum unanimous then "A", otherwise "B", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not is sign flip, allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".
Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
Σ Danger Tier Count Model Summary Σ Caution Tier Count Model Summary Σ Safe Tier Count Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 11 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Stratum Summary ● Stratum Label Case Cell ● Stratum Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Stratum Summary ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

DANGER when DistortionType is A or B, CAUTION when C+/C-, SAFE when D. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio).

ƒ Arm Size Ratio Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-219
In RuleSpeak

The treatment ranking's arm size ratio is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the total cases a plus the total cases b is 0
  2. the total cases a divided by the total cases a plus the total cases b in all other cases
In plain words

If (total cases a plus total cases b) equals 0 then 0, otherwise (total cases a divided by (total cases a plus total cases b)).

As a formula
= IF(Total Cases a + Total Cases B = 0, 0, Total Cases a / (Total Cases a + Total Cases B))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣTotal Cases aTreatment Ranking Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment a. ΣTotal Cases BTreatment Ranking Sum of cases across all case cells whose study is study and treatment label is treatment b.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Symmetry Departure Treatment Ranking
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

TotalCasesA / (TotalCasesA + TotalCasesB) — the share of total cases allocated to TreatmentA. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio for why the empty-string zero-denominator idiom is unsafe on a numeric field). Returns 0 for the degenerate zero-case-count edge (never occurs in this corpus, kept for defensive symmetry with DistortionRatio).

ƒ Symmetry Departure Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-220
In RuleSpeak

A treatment ranking's symmetry departure is computed as the absolute value of the arm size ratio minus 0.5.

In plain words

The absolute value of (arm size ratio minus 0.5).

As a formula
= ABS(Arm Size Ratio - 0.5)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒArm Size RatioTreatment Ranking If (total cases a plus total cases b) equals 0 then 0, otherwise (total cases a divided by (total cases a plus total cases b)).
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ⇢ Study Case Cell ⇢ Study Treatment Ranking ● Treatment Label Case Cell ● Treatment a Treatment Ranking ● Treatment B Treatment Ranking

ABS(ArmSizeRatio - 0.5) — how far the case allocation departs from a balanced 50/50 split. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio).

Invariant Check

An invariant check is identified by its name.

● Algebraic Statement Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The mathematical assertion in compact symbolic form.

● Natural Language Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-English statement of what property must hold and why it is an invariant of the model.

● Source Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The DAG table whose rows are checked (TreatmentRankings or StratumSummaries).

● Filter Expression Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Condition selecting the universe of rows this invariant applies to. Empty means all rows.

● Assertion Expression Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The per-row condition that must be TRUE for every row in scope.

● SQL Filter Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Optional SQL WHERE clause that selects the universe of rows in scope for this invariant (e.g. distortion_type = 'D'). Empty string means all rows.

● SQL Assertion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The SQL WHERE clause (without WHERE keyword) that counts PASSING rows from vw_treatment_rankings for this invariant. Used by run_invariant_checks() to evaluate each assertion dynamically from the rulebook table.

● Pass Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Universe Count Invariant Check

Rows where the invariant holds. Populated by 05b-customize-data.sql from a live query against vw_treatment_rankings.

● Fail Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Universe Count Invariant Check ƒ Is Green Invariant Check ƒ Status Label Invariant Check

Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

ƒ Universe Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-221
In RuleSpeak

An invariant check's universe count is computed as the pass count plus the fail count.

In plain words

Pass count plus fail count.

As a formula
= Pass Count + Fail Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Pass CountInvariant Check Rows where the invariant holds. Populated by 05b-customize-data.sql from a live query against vw_treatment_rankings. ●Fail CountInvariant Check Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Pass Count Invariant Check ● Fail Count Invariant Check

Total rows in scope (PassCount + FailCount).

ƒ Is Green Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-222
In RuleSpeak

An invariant check is considered a green if the fail count is 0.

In plain words

Fail count equals 0.

As a formula
= Fail Count = 0
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Fail CountInvariant Check Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Status Label Invariant Check
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Fail Count Invariant Check

TRUE when FailCount = 0.

ƒ Status Label Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-223
In RuleSpeak

The invariant check's status label is determined by the following priority:

  1. “PASS” if the green flag is set
  2. “FAIL(”, followed by the fail count, followed by “)” in all other cases
In plain words

If is green then "PASS", otherwise "FAIL(", fail count, joined with ")".

As a formula
= IF(Is Green, "PASS", CONCAT("FAIL(", Fail Count, ")"))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs GreenInvariant Check Fail count equals 0. ●Fail CountInvariant Check Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Fail Count Invariant Check

PASS or FAIL(n) summary for display.

● Severity Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

critical | warning — critical means any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

● Protects Conclusion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
↗ Protects Conclusion Title Invariant Check ↗ Protects Conclusion Category Invariant Check Σ Invariant Protecting Count Conclusion

FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined.

↗ Tradition Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-224
In RuleSpeak

An invariant check's tradition name is the value of LOOKUP(TraditionId, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{TraditionId}}, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Tradition ID, ResearchTraditions[TraditionId], ResearchTraditions[Name])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDInvariant Check FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical commitments this algebraic invariant protects — a failed invariant would falsify that tradition's key claim.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Invariant Check

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

Materialized Entity

A materialized entity is identified by its name.

● Entity Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The rulebook table whose fields are materialized (e.g. SweepStudySummary).

● Refresh Contract Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

eager-on-write | on-demand | scheduled — when the refresh function is invoked relative to writes on the upstream data.

● Refresh Function Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The SQL function (in the customize-* override files) that recomputes this entity's materialized fields from its upstream SSoT tables.

● Upstream Tables Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Comma-separated list of the tables this materialization is derived from — its sealed/stable upstream.

● Justification Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The measured cost or evidence that justified materializing this entity instead of leaving it as a live calculated/aggregation field.

Methodology

A methodology is identified by its name.

● Phase Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Broad phase: axiom | decomposition | derivation | hydration | validation | taxonomy | conclusion

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Short title of the methodological principle or step.

● Statement Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

One-to-three sentence statement of the principle, precise enough to be applied or falsified.

● Why It Matters Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Why this step or principle is load-bearing for this model — what breaks if you skip it.

● Illustrated by Loop Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

LoopId(s) where this principle was concretely demonstrated (comma-separated).

● Pioneering Researcher Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
↗ Pioneering Researcher Name Methodology ↗ Pioneering Researcher Affiliation Methodology

FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with developing or popularising this methodological principle in the Simpson's Paradox context.

Σ Loop Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-232
In RuleSpeak

A methodology's loop count is the number of loops related to the methodology.

In plain words

Count of loops whose tradition ID is tradition ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDLoop FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development. ●Tradition IDMethodology FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition that introduced or most relies on this methodological approach.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Tradition ID Loop ● Tradition ID Methodology

Count of Loops whose TraditionId matches this methodology entry's TraditionId — how many build iterations this methodological tradition has driven.

Conclusion

A conclusion is identified by its name.

● Category Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Theorem Count Model Summary Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary

domain | instrument | methodology | open-question | corpus-theorem

● Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Theorem Count Model Summary Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary

witnessed | planned | open

● Report Tier Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

critical | supporting | transient — controls how much space this conclusion gets in the generated PDF/summary report. critical = full section with evidence + chart; supporting = 1-2 line bullet; transient = folded into a single compact list.

● Validating Hypothesis Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
↗ Live Status Confirmed Conclusion ↗ Live Status Observed Metric Conclusion

FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId. The pre-registered hypothesis whose live DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed value is the current-truth check for this conclusion — set on theorem/corpus-theorem conclusions so a report can distinguish a conclusion's archived prose (may say 'Proved') from its live status (may since be contradicted by corpus growth). Null when no single hypothesis tests this exact claim.

↗ Live Status Confirmed Lookup 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-233
In RuleSpeak

A conclusion's live status confirmed is the value of LOOKUP(ValidatingHypothesis, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[IsConfirmed]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{ValidatingHypothesis}}, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[IsConfirmed]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Validating Hypothesis, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[IsConfirmed])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Validating HypothesisConclusion FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId. The pre-registered hypothesis whose live DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed value is the current-truth check for this conclusion — set on theorem/corpus-theorem conclusions so a report can distinguish a conclusion's archived prose (may say 'Proved') from its live status (may since be contradicted by corpus growth). Null when no single hypothesis tests this exact claim.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Validating Hypothesis Conclusion

Lookup: DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed via ValidatingHypothesis — the current pass/fail state of the hypothesis backing this conclusion, recomputed on every build from live corpus data. NULL when ValidatingHypothesis is not set (no live check exists for this conclusion).

↗ Live Status Observed Metric Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-234
In RuleSpeak

A conclusion's live status observed metric is the value of LOOKUP(ValidatingHypothesis, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[ObservedMetric]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{ValidatingHypothesis}}, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[ObservedMetric]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Validating Hypothesis, DiscoveryFindings[HypothesisId], DiscoveryFindings[ObservedMetric])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Validating HypothesisConclusion FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId. The pre-registered hypothesis whose live DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed value is the current-truth check for this conclusion — set on theorem/corpus-theorem conclusions so a report can distinguish a conclusion's archived prose (may say 'Proved') from its live status (may since be contradicted by corpus growth). Null when no single hypothesis tests this exact claim.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Validating Hypothesis Conclusion

Lookup: DiscoveryFindings.ObservedMetric via ValidatingHypothesis — the current observed values for the hypothesis backing this conclusion, so a reader can see exactly why it currently passes or fails.

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

One-line statement of the conclusion.

● Evidence Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The specific DB query result, loop outcome, or argument that establishes or will establish this conclusion.

● Witnessed in Loop Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 5 direct rules that read this one
↗ Witnessed in Loop Title Conclusion ↗ Witnessed in Loop Commit Hash Conclusion ↗ Witnessed in Loop Commit Short Conclusion ↗ Witnessed in Loop Commit Date Conclusion ↗ Witnessed in Loop Git Tag Conclusion

LoopId where this conclusion was first witnessed (if status=witnessed).

● Target Loop Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

LoopId that is designed to witness this conclusion (if status=planned).

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. A researcher whose position this conclusion refutes or qualifies (e.g. conc-03 refutes Blyth's unanimity criterion as the primary definition).

UI Screen

A UI screen is identified by its name.

● Title Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable screen title shown in the UI.

● Route Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

URL path pattern (e.g. /, /study/:studyId, /compare).

● Primary Entity Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The main rulebook entity this screen reads from (the vw_* view name without prefix).

● Purpose Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

One sentence: what a user can learn or do on this screen.

● Key Fields Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Comma-separated list of the most important fields displayed on this screen.

● User Action Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Primary user action available on this screen (e.g. select a study, drill into a stratum).

● Primary Conclusion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one

● Tradition ID UI Screen

Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId — the lens this screen applies.

↗ Primary Conclusion Title Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-248
In RuleSpeak

A UI screen's primary conclusion title is the value of LOOKUP(PrimaryConclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{PrimaryConclusion}}, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Primary Conclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Title])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Primary ConclusionUI Screen FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion this screen most directly visualises or communicates to the user.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Primary Conclusion UI Screen

Lookup: Conclusions.Title via PrimaryConclusion.

↗ Primary Conclusion Evidence Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-249
In RuleSpeak

A UI screen's primary conclusion evidence is the value of LOOKUP(PrimaryConclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Evidence]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{PrimaryConclusion}}, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Evidence]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Primary Conclusion, Conclusions[ConclusionId], Conclusions[Evidence])
