UI Component

A UI component is identified by its name.

● Component Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Code-level component name (PascalCase, e.g. DistortionBadge).

● Used on Screens Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Comma-separated ScreenIds where this component appears.

● Primary Entity Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The rulebook entity this component reads (the vw_* view).

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What this component renders and what decisions a developer must make to implement it.

● Required Fields Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Comma-separated rulebook fields this component must receive as props.

● Visual Spec Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-English description of the visual output: shape, color coding, interactivity.

Instrument Spec

An instrument spec is identified by its name.

● Component Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

input | derived | classification | adapter-requirement

● Field Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The field or variable name in the DAG.

● Data Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

integer | decimal | boolean | string | categorical

● Source Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The DAG table where this field is computed or stored.

● Formula Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The ERB formula or derivation rule for this component.

● Natural Language Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-English statement of what this component represents or requires.

● Adapter Note Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What a researcher must supply or verify to satisfy this component when hydrating a new study.

Allocation Sweep

An allocation sweep is identified by its name.

● Alloc Fraction a Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct +6 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Is Original Allocation Sweep ƒ N Sweep a Allocation Sweep N Sweep B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep

Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study.

ƒ Is Original Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-250
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep is considered an original if the value of ABS(AllocFractionA - LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03.

In plain words

ABS({{AllocFractionA}} - LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03.

As a formula
= ABS(Alloc Fraction a - LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[OriginalAllocFractionA])) < 0.03
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Alloc Fraction aAllocation Sweep Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study. ●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep ● Study ID Allocation Sweep

TRUE for the row closest to the actual original study allocation.

ƒ N Sweep Stratum Total Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-251
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's n sweep stratum total is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 2 direct +5 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ N Sweep a Allocation Sweep ƒ N Sweep B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Total cases in the sweep stratum (the stratum whose allocation is being varied). Fixed per study.

ƒ N Sweep a Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-252
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's n sweep a is computed as the n sweep stratum total times the alloc fraction a rounded to the nearest whole number.

In plain words

(n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number.

As a formula
= ROUND(N Sweep Stratum Total * Alloc Fraction a)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒN Sweep Stratum TotalAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]). ●Alloc Fraction aAllocation Sweep Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study.
Consumers 2 direct +4 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ N Sweep B Allocation Sweep ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment A = ROUND(NSweepStratumTotal * AllocFractionA).

ƒ N Sweep B Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-253
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's n sweep b is computed as the n sweep stratum total minus the n sweep a.

In plain words

N sweep stratum total minus n sweep a.

As a formula
= N Sweep Stratum Total - N Sweep a
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒN Sweep Stratum TotalAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NSweepStratumTotal]). ƒN Sweep aAllocation Sweep (n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment B = NSweepStratumTotal - NSweepA.

ƒ N Fixed a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-254
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's n fixed a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Total A-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 87 (small-stratum A). For berkeley-1973 = 1302 (depts B+C+D male). For compressed-synthetic = 80 (easy-stratum A). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum A).

ƒ N Fixed B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-255
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's n fixed b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Total B-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 270 (small-stratum B). For berkeley-1973 = 993 (depts B+C+D female). For compressed-synthetic = 120 (easy-stratum B). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum B).

ƒ Sweep Rate a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-256
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's sweep rate a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Per-cell success rate for treatment A in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

ƒ Sweep Rate B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-257
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's sweep rate b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Per-cell success rate for treatment B in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

ƒ Fixed Rate a Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-258
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's fixed rate a is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Weighted success rate for treatment A across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

ƒ Fixed Rate B Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-259
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's fixed rate b is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Weighted success rate for treatment B across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate a Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-260
In RuleSpeak

The allocation sweep's sweep pooled rate a is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the n sweep a plus the n fixed a is 0
  2. the n sweep a times the sweep rate a plus the n fixed a times the fixed rate a divided by the n sweep a plus the n fixed a in all other cases
In plain words

If (n sweep a plus n fixed a) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep a times sweep rate a) plus (n fixed a times fixed rate a)) divided by (n sweep a plus n fixed a)).

As a formula
= IF(N Sweep a + N Fixed a = 0, "", (N Sweep a * Sweep Rate a + N Fixed a * Fixed Rate a) / (N Sweep a + N Fixed a))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒN Sweep aAllocation Sweep (n sweep stratum total times alloc fraction a) rounded to the nearest whole number. ƒN Fixed aAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedA]). ƒSweep Rate aAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateA]). ƒFixed Rate aAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateA]).
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

Pooled success rate for treatment A = (NSweepA * SweepRateA + NFixedA * FixedRateA) / (NSweepA + NFixedA). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

ƒ Sweep Pooled Rate B Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-261
In RuleSpeak

The allocation sweep's sweep pooled rate b is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the n sweep b plus the n fixed b is 0
  2. the n sweep b times the sweep rate b plus the n fixed b times the fixed rate b divided by the n sweep b plus the n fixed b in all other cases
In plain words

If (n sweep b plus n fixed b) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep b times sweep rate b) plus (n fixed b times fixed rate b)) divided by (n sweep b plus n fixed b)).

As a formula
= IF(N Sweep B + N Fixed B = 0, "", (N Sweep B * Sweep Rate B + N Fixed B * Fixed Rate B) / (N Sweep B + N Fixed B))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒN Sweep BAllocation Sweep N sweep stratum total minus n sweep a. ƒN Fixed BAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[NFixedB]). ƒSweep Rate BAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepRateB]). ƒFixed Rate BAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[FixedRateB]).
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Allocation Sweep Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

Pooled success rate for treatment B = (NSweepB * SweepRateB + NFixedB * FixedRateB) / (NSweepB + NFixedB). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

ƒ Sweep Pooled Gap Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-262
In RuleSpeak

The allocation sweep's sweep pooled gap is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the sweep pooled rate a is blank
  2. the sweep pooled rate a minus the sweep pooled rate b in all other cases
In plain words

If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b).

As a formula
= IF(Sweep Pooled Rate a = "", "", Sweep Pooled Rate a - Sweep Pooled Rate B)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Pooled Rate aAllocation Sweep If (n sweep a plus n fixed a) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep a times sweep rate a) plus (n fixed a times fixed rate a)) divided by (n sweep a plus n fixed a)). ƒSweep Pooled Rate BAllocation Sweep If (n sweep b plus n fixed b) equals 0 then "", otherwise (((n sweep b times sweep rate b) plus (n fixed b times fixed rate b)) divided by (n sweep b plus n fixed b)).
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
Σ Sweep Pooled Gap Range Model Summary ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

SweepPooledRateA minus SweepPooledRateB. Wanders as AllocFractionA varies because pooled rates mix stratum sizes unequally. Crosses zero at some allocation (the paradox reversal point).

ƒ Sweep Corrected Gap Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-263
In RuleSpeak

An allocation sweep's sweep corrected gap is computed as the value of LOOKUP(StudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Study IDAllocation Sweep The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.
Consumers 3 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Max Model Summary Σ Sweep Corrected Gap Min Model Summary ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness Allocation Sweep Sweep Corrected Gap Range Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep

Allocation-free signal: sum over strata of (n_s/N) * (rate_As - rate_Bs). Constant across the sweep by construction — the allocation variable does not appear. This is the conserved quantity under re-stratification.

ƒ Allocation Distortion Witness Calculated 6 hops from ground truth number DR-264
In RuleSpeak

The allocation sweep's allocation distortion witness is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the sweep pooled gap is blank
  2. the sweep pooled gap minus the sweep corrected gap in all other cases
In plain words

If sweep pooled gap is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled gap minus sweep corrected gap).

As a formula
= IF(Sweep Pooled Gap = "", "", Sweep Pooled Gap - Sweep Corrected Gap)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒSweep Pooled GapAllocation Sweep If sweep pooled rate a is "" then "", otherwise (sweep pooled rate a minus sweep pooled rate b). ƒSweep Corrected GapAllocation Sweep LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepCorrectedGap]).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Allocation Sweep ● Alloc Fraction a Allocation Sweep

SweepPooledGap minus SweepCorrectedGap. The allocation distortion: how much the pooled signal has been inflated or deflated by differential stratum allocation. Zero only when allocation is balanced.

Sweep Study Summary

A sweep study summary is identified by its name.

ƒ Distortion Type Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-265
In RuleSpeak

A sweep study summary's distortion type label is computed as the value of LOOKUP(SweepStudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{SweepStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Sweep Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Sweep Study IDSweep Study Summary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Sweep Study ID Sweep Study Summary

DistortionType for this study (A, B, C, or D) — provides context for what the sweep should show.

● Corrected Gap Constant Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary(), 03b/05b customize SQL). The allocation-free corrected gap for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). Upstream (AllocationSweep, SweepStudyConfig) is sealed/stable per build; recomputing this per-row via nested calc_* calls was measured to fan out to 22.5s for a 242-study corpus scan with zero memoization — materialized here instead of left as a live calculated field. See MaterializedEntities.

● Sweep Corrected Gap Max Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MAXIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Sweep Corrected Gap Min Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Sweep Corrected Gap Range Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
Σ Max Study Sweep Corrected Gap Range Model Summary Σ Corrected Gap Invariant Fail Count Model Summary ƒ Invariant Witness Sweep Study Summary

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = SweepCorrectedGapMax - SweepCorrectedGapMin. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Sweep Pooled Gap Max Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Maximum SweepPooledGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MAXIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepPooledGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Sweep Pooled Gap Min Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Minimum SweepPooledGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepPooledGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Sweep Pooled Gap Range Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepPooledGap across the sweep = SweepPooledGapMax - SweepPooledGapMin. Should be large for Type A/B studies (pooled gap wanders) and smaller for Type D (balanced allocation = less wandering). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

● Pooled Gap Crosses Zero Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). TRUE when SweepPooledGapMin < 0 and SweepPooledGapMax > 0 — the pooled gap changes sign somewhere in the sweep, meaning the paradox can be created or destroyed by pure allocation. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

ƒ Invariant Witness Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-266
In RuleSpeak

The sweep study summary's invariant witness is determined by the following priority:

  1. “PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep” if the sweep corrected gap range is less than 0.0001
  2. “FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error” in all other cases
In plain words

If sweep corrected gap range is less than 0.0001 then "PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep", otherwise "FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error".

As a formula
= IF(Sweep Corrected Gap Range < 0.0001, "PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep", "FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Sweep Corrected Gap RangeSweep Study Summary Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = SweepCorrectedGapMax - SweepCorrectedGapMin. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Sweep Corrected Gap Range Sweep Study Summary

Summary witness statement: PASS if CorrectedGap is invariant (range < 0.0001), FAIL otherwise.

ƒ Sweep Stratum Label Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-267
In RuleSpeak

A sweep study summary's sweep stratum label is computed as the value of LOOKUP(SweepStudyId, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{SweepStudyId}}, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Sweep Study ID, SweepStudyConfig[StudyId], SweepStudyConfig[SweepStratumLabel])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Sweep Study IDSweep Study Summary FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Sweep Study ID Sweep Study Summary

Which stratum is being swept for this study.

Research Tradition

A research tradition is identified by its name.

● Era Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Approximate active period, e.g. "1899–1972".

● Core Concern Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The central question this tradition asks about the paradox.

● Primary Venue Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Where this tradition publishes (journal, conference, book series).

● Key Claim Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The tradition's signature thesis or resolution strategy.

● Open Question Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What this tradition leaves unresolved or contested.

● Verified by Deep Research Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed the tradition at high confidence.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Σ Researcher Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-273
In RuleSpeak

A research tradition's researcher count is the number of researchers related to the research tradition.

In plain words

Count of researchers whose tradition ID is tradition ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Tradition IDResearcher FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId ●Tradition IDResearch Tradition Slug identifier, e.g. tradition-historical.

In RuleSpeak

A research tradition's study count is the number of studies related to the research tradition.

In plain words

Count of studies whose tradition ID is tradition ID.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Tradition ID, Tradition ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads

Researcher

A researcher is identified by its name.

● Active Period Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Approximate years of relevant work, e.g. "1899".

● Affiliation Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Primary institutional affiliation for the relevant work.

● Key Contribution Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

One-sentence description of their specific contribution to the paradox literature.

● Signature Work Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

The canonical paper or book most associated with their contribution.

● Relation to Hannan Pearl Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

How this researcher's line of work relates to or differs from Hannan (aggregation bias) and Pearl (DAG/do-calculus).

● Verified by Deep Research Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed this person at high confidence.

● Confidence Level Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

high | medium | unverified — matches deep-research vote outcome.

● Illustrates Conclusion Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Synthetic Phase

A synthetic phase is identified by its name.

● Param Stratum Fraction Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct +12 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase S1 Total Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Corrected Gap Synthetic Phase Phase NA1 Synthetic Phase Phase NB1 Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase NA2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase NB2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase

w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid.

● Param Stratum Gap1 Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 4 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Rate A1 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Rate B1 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Corrected Gap Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase

g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1.

● Param Stratum Gap2 Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 4 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Rate A2 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Rate B2 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Corrected Gap Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase

g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.

● Param Allocation Bias Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +11 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase NA1 Synthetic Phase Phase NB1 Synthetic Phase Phase NA2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase NB2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase

b — allocation bias in stratum 1: extra fraction of stratum-1 cases assigned to treatment A above 50/50. Range 0.00–0.95.

ƒ Phase S1 Total Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-283
In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase s1 total is computed as 200 times the param stratum fraction rounded to the nearest whole number.

In plain words

(200 times param stratum fraction) rounded to the nearest whole number.

As a formula
= ROUND(200 * Param Stratum Fraction)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Param Stratum FractionSynthetic Phase w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid.
Consumers 2 direct +10 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase NA1 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase NB1 Synthetic Phase Phase NA2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase NB2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase

Total cases in stratum 1 = ROUND(200 × ParamStratumFraction).

ƒ Phase NA1 Calculated 2 hops from ground truth number DR-284
In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase NA1 is computed as the phase s1 total times 0.5 plus the param allocation bias divided by 2 rounded to the nearest whole number.

In plain words

(phase s1 total times (0.5 plus (param allocation bias divided by 2))) rounded to the nearest whole number.

As a formula
= ROUND(Phase S1 Total * (0.5 + Param Allocation Bias / 2))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase S1 TotalSynthetic Phase (200 times param stratum fraction) rounded to the nearest whole number. ●Param Allocation BiasSynthetic Phase b — allocation bias in stratum 1: extra fraction of stratum-1 cases assigned to treatment A above 50/50. Range 0.00–0.95.
Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase NB1 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase NA2 Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Pooled Rate a Synthetic Phase Phase NB2 Synthetic Phase Phase Pooled Rate B Synthetic Phase Phase Signed Pooled Gap Synthetic Phase Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase

= Phase S1 Total - Phase NA1

In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase pooled rate a is computed as the phase NA1 times the phase rate a1 plus the phase NA2 times the phase rate a2 divided by 100.

As a formula
= (Phase NA1 * Phase Rate A1 + Phase NA2 * Phase Rate A2) / 100

In RuleSpeak

As a formula
= Phase Pooled Rate a - Phase Pooled Rate B
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase Pooled Rate aSynthetic Phase ((phase NA1 times phase rate a1) plus (phase NA2 times phase rate a2)) divided by 100. ƒPhase Pooled Rate BSynthetic Phase ((phase NB1 times phase rate b1) plus (phase NB2 times phase rate b2)) divided by 100.
Consumers 4 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase

Pooled rate gap (A minus B).

ƒ Phase Corrected Gap Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-295
In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase corrected gap is computed as the param stratum fraction times the param stratum gap1 plus 1 minus the param stratum fraction times the param stratum gap2.

In plain words

(param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2).

As a formula
= Param Stratum Fraction * Param Stratum Gap1 + (1 - Param Stratum Fraction) * Param Stratum Gap2
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●Param Stratum FractionSynthetic Phase w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid. ●Param Stratum Gap1Synthetic Phase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1. ●Param Stratum Gap2Synthetic Phase g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.
Consumers 3 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Phase is Sign Flip Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion Synthetic Phase ƒ Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase

Allocation-free equal-weight gap = w×g1 + (1−w)×g2.

ƒ Phase Strata Won by Loser Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-296
In RuleSpeak

The synthetic phase's phase strata won by loser is determined by the following priority:

  1. the count of the following that hold: the param stratum gap1 is less than 0 and the param stratum gap2 is less than 0 if the phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0
  2. the count of the following that hold: the param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 and the param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 in all other cases
In plain words

If phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 then (if param stratum gap1 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0), otherwise (if param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0).

As a formula
= IF(Phase Signed Pooled Gap > 0, IF(Param Stratum Gap1 < 0, 1, 0) + IF(Param Stratum Gap2 < 0, 1, 0), IF(Param Stratum Gap1 > 0, 1, 0) + IF(Param Stratum Gap2 > 0, 1, 0))
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSynthetic Phase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b. ●Param Stratum Gap1Synthetic Phase g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1. ●Param Stratum Gap2Synthetic Phase g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Reversal Intensity Synthetic Phase Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase

Count of strata whose per-stratum winner disagrees with the pooled winner.

ƒ Phase Reversal Intensity Calculated 8 hops from ground truth number DR-297
In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase reversal intensity is computed as the phase strata won by loser divided by 2.

In plain words

Phase strata won by loser divided by 2.

As a formula
= Phase Strata Won by Loser / 2
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase Strata Won by LoserSynthetic Phase If phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 then (if param stratum gap1 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is less than 0 then 1, otherwise 0), otherwise (if param stratum gap1 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0 plus if param stratum gap2 is greater than 0 then 1, otherwise 0).
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase

PhaseStrataWonByLoser / 2 for this 2-stratum template.

ƒ Phase is Sign Flip Calculated 7 hops from ground truth boolean DR-298
In RuleSpeak

The synthetic phase's phase is sign flip is determined by the following priority:

  1. the phase signed pooled gap is less than 0 if the phase corrected gap is greater than 0
  2. the phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0 in all other cases
In plain words

If phase corrected gap is greater than 0 then phase signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0.

As a formula
= IF(Phase Corrected Gap > 0, Phase Signed Pooled Gap < 0, Phase Signed Pooled Gap > 0)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase Corrected GapSynthetic Phase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSynthetic Phase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase

TRUE when equal-weight and pooled gaps have opposite signs.

ƒ Phase Allocation Distortion Calculated 7 hops from ground truth number DR-299
In RuleSpeak

A synthetic phase's phase allocation distortion is computed as the absolute value of the phase corrected gap minus the phase signed pooled gap.

In plain words

The absolute value of (phase corrected gap minus phase signed pooled gap).

As a formula
= ABS(Phase Corrected Gap - Phase Signed Pooled Gap)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase Corrected GapSynthetic Phase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSynthetic Phase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Distortion Type Synthetic Phase
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase

|PhaseCorrectedGap − PhaseSignedPooledGap|.

ƒ Phase Distortion Type Calculated 9 hops from ground truth string DR-300
In RuleSpeak

The synthetic phase's phase distortion type is determined by the following priority:

  1. “A” if all of these hold:
    • the phase is sign flip flag is set
    • the phase reversal intensity is 1
  2. “B” if all of these hold:
    • the phase is sign flip flag is set
    • the phase reversal intensity is less than 1
  3. “C+” if all of these hold:
    • the phase is sign flip flag is not set
    • the phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
    • the absolute value of the phase signed pooled gap is greater than the absolute value of the phase corrected gap plus 0.001
  4. “C-” if all of these hold:
    • the phase is sign flip flag is not set
    • the phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01
    • the absolute value of the phase signed pooled gap is less than the absolute value of the phase corrected gap minus 0.001
  5. “D” in all other cases
In plain words

If phase is sign flip and phase reversal intensity equals 1 then "A", otherwise if phase is sign flip and phase reversal intensity is less than 1 then "B", otherwise if not phase is sign flip, phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of phase signed pooled gap is greater than (the absolute value of phase corrected gap plus 0.001) then "C+", otherwise if not phase is sign flip, phase allocation distortion is greater than 0.01, and the absolute value of phase signed pooled gap is less than (the absolute value of phase corrected gap minus 0.001) then "C-", otherwise "D".

As a formula
= IF(AND(Phase is Sign Flip, Phase Reversal Intensity = 1), "A", IF(AND(Phase is Sign Flip, Phase Reversal Intensity < 1), "B", IF(AND(NOT(Phase is Sign Flip), Phase Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Phase Signed Pooled Gap) > ABS(Phase Corrected Gap) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT(Phase is Sign Flip), Phase Allocation Distortion > 0.01, ABS(Phase Signed Pooled Gap) < ABS(Phase Corrected Gap) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒPhase is Sign FlipSynthetic Phase If phase corrected gap is greater than 0 then phase signed pooled gap is less than 0, otherwise phase signed pooled gap is greater than 0. ƒPhase Reversal IntensitySynthetic Phase Phase strata won by loser divided by 2. ƒPhase Allocation DistortionSynthetic Phase The absolute value of (phase corrected gap minus phase signed pooled gap). ƒPhase Signed Pooled GapSynthetic Phase Phase pooled rate a minus phase pooled rate b. ƒPhase Corrected GapSynthetic Phase (param stratum fraction times param stratum gap1) plus ((1 minus param stratum fraction) times param stratum gap2).
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Param Stratum Fraction Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap1 Synthetic Phase ● Param Stratum Gap2 Synthetic Phase ● Param Allocation Bias Synthetic Phase

Five-type geometric classification at this parameter point.

Phase Diagram Summary

A phase diagram summary is identified by its name.

ƒ Phase Point Count Calculated 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-301
In RuleSpeak

A phase diagram summary's phase point count is computed as the phase type a count plus the phase type b count plus the phase type c plus count plus the phase type c minus count plus the phase type d count.

In plain words

(((phase type a count plus phase type b count) plus phase type c plus count) plus phase type c minus count) plus phase type d count.

As a formula
= Phase Type a Count + Phase Type B Count + Phase Type C Plus Count + Phase Type C Minus Count + Phase Type D Count
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●Phase Type a CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Phase Type a Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type B Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Plus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Minus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type D Count Phase Diagram Summary

Total grid points in SyntheticPhase (sum of type counts). Refreshed from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

● Phase Type a Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Point Count Phase Diagram Summary ƒ All Five Types Present Phase Diagram Summary ƒ Phase Taxonomy Witness Phase Diagram Summary Phase Witness Note Phase Diagram Summary

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Phase Type C Minus Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one

Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

● Phase Type D Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 3 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Point Count Phase Diagram Summary ƒ All Five Types Present Phase Diagram Summary ƒ Phase Taxonomy Witness Phase Diagram Summary Phase Witness Note Phase Diagram Summary

Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

ƒ All Five Types Present Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-302
In RuleSpeak

A phase diagram summary is flagged all five types present if all of the following:

    • the phase type a count is greater than 0
    • the phase type b count is greater than 0
    • the phase type c plus count is greater than 0
    • the phase type c minus count is greater than 0
    • the phase type d count is greater than 0
In plain words

Phase type a count is greater than 0, phase type b count is greater than 0, phase type c plus count is greater than 0, phase type c minus count is greater than 0, and phase type d count is greater than 0.

As a formula
= AND(Phase Type a Count > 0, Phase Type B Count > 0, Phase Type C Plus Count > 0, Phase Type C Minus Count > 0, Phase Type D Count > 0)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●Phase Type a CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Phase Witness Note Phase Diagram Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Phase Type a Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type B Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Plus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Minus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type D Count Phase Diagram Summary

TRUE when every DistortionType cell has at least one grid point.

ƒ Phase Taxonomy Witness Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-303
In RuleSpeak

A phase diagram summary's phase taxonomy witness is computed as “A:”, followed by the phase type a count, followed by “ B:”, followed by the phase type b count, followed by “ C+:”, followed by the phase type c plus count, followed by “ C-:”, followed by the phase type c minus count, followed by “ D:”, followed by the phase type d count.

In plain words

"A:", phase type a count, " B:", phase type b count, " C+:", phase type c plus count, " C-:", phase type c minus count, " D:", joined with phase type d count.

As a formula
= CONCAT("A:", Phase Type a Count, " B:", Phase Type B Count, " C+:", Phase Type C Plus Count, " C-:", Phase Type C Minus Count, " D:", Phase Type D Count)
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
●Phase Type a CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type B CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Plus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type C Minus CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql. ●Phase Type D CountPhase Diagram Summary Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Phase Type a Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type B Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Plus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Minus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type D Count Phase Diagram Summary

Human-readable witness string of type counts across the grid.

ƒ Phase Witness Note Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-304
In RuleSpeak

The phase diagram summary's phase witness note is determined by the following priority:

  1. “PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space” if the all five types present flag is set
  2. “FAIL: missing distortion type in grid” in all other cases
In plain words

If all five types present then "PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space", otherwise "FAIL: missing distortion type in grid".

As a formula
= IF(All Five Types Present, "PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space", "FAIL: missing distortion type in grid")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒAll Five Types PresentPhase Diagram Summary Phase type a count is greater than 0, phase type b count is greater than 0, phase type c plus count is greater than 0, phase type c minus count is greater than 0, and phase type d count is greater than 0.
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Phase Type a Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type B Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Plus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type C Minus Count Phase Diagram Summary ● Phase Type D Count Phase Diagram Summary

PASS/FAIL summary for taxonomy completeness.

Ingestion Protocol

An ingestion protocol is identified by its name.

● Rule Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

required-input | structural | inclusion | exclusion | adapter-metadata

● Target Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Rulebook table this item applies to.

● Field Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Column name when RuleType=required-input.

● Requirement Statement Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Plain-English statement of the requirement.

● Mechanical Check Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

How to verify compliance before classification.

● Sort Order Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Display order in the ingestion checklist.

Ingestion Summary

An ingestion summary is identified by its name.

Σ Protocol Item Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-305
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's protocol item count is the number of ingestion protocol related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count(protocol ID).

As a formula
= COUNT(Protocol ID)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Protocol IDIngestion Protocol Unique slug for this contract item.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Protocol ID Ingestion Protocol

Total contract items. Fixed loop-90: COUNTIFS(...,"<>") mistranslates to a literal '<>' string-equality filter (a known transpiler bug) instead of IS NOT NULL, always returning 0; replaced with bare COUNT(), which transpiles correctly.

Σ Corpus Cell Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-306
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's corpus cell count is the number of case cells related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count(case cell ID).

As a formula
= COUNT(Case Cell ID)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Case Cell IDCase Cell Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-small-A).
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Cells Valid Ingestion Summary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Case Cell ID Case Cell

Total CaseCell rows. Fixed loop-90 (see ProtocolItemCount).

Σ Valid Cell Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-307
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's valid cell count is the number of case cells related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count of case cells whose is valid ingestion cell is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Valid Ingestion Cell, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Valid Ingestion CellCase Cell Cases is at least 0, successes is at least 0, and successes is at most cases.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Cells Valid Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell

Valid CaseCells.

ƒ All Cells Valid Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-308
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary is flagged all cells valid if the valid cell count is the corpus cell count.

In plain words

Valid cell count equals corpus cell count.

As a formula
= Valid Cell Count = Corpus Cell Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣValid Cell CountIngestion Summary Count of case cells whose is valid ingestion cell is true(). ΣCorpus Cell CountIngestion Summary Count(case cell ID).
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Case Cell ID Case Cell

Every cell passes validity predicate.

Σ Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-309
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's study count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count of studies whose is control study is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Control Study, FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Is Control StudyStudy TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Studies Structural Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Control Study Study

Count of studies subject to the ingestion contract (excludes control studies, loop-92 -- control studies were never encoded with paired treatment arms, so IngestionCellParity is structurally undefined for them, not a real ingestion defect).

Σ Structural Compliant Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-310
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's structural compliant count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count of studies whose ingestion cell parity is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Ingestion Cell Parity, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIngestion Cell ParityStudy AND(LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) >= 2, {{CellCount}} = LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[StratumCount]) * 2).
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Studies Structural Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell

Studies with IngestionCellParity.

ƒ All Studies Structural Calculated 4 hops from ground truth boolean DR-311
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary is flagged all studies structural if the structural compliant count is the study count.

In plain words

Structural compliant count equals study count.

As a formula
= Structural Compliant Count = Study Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣStructural Compliant CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose ingestion cell parity is true(). ΣStudy CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose is control study is false().
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Is Control Study Study

All studies structurally valid.

Σ Real Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-312
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's real study count is the number of the ingestion summary's studies that are not control studies.

In plain words

Count of studies whose is synthetic is false() and is control study is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Synthetic, FALSE(), Is Control Study, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims. ●Is Control StudyStudy TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Is Control Study Study

Non-synthetic, non-control studies (loop-92 -- control studies were never encoded with paired treatment arms, so they are outside the ingestion contract's scope, same as StructuralCompliantCount's StudyCount).

Σ Real Fully Compliant Count Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-313
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary's real fully compliant count is the number of studies related to the ingestion summary.

In plain words

Count of studies whose ingestion compliance is "all".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Ingestion Compliance, "all")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIngestion ComplianceStudy IF({{IsSynthetic}}, "exempt-synthetic", IF(AND({{IngestionCellParity}}, LOOKUP({{StudyId}}, StratumVariables[Study], StratumVariables[VariableName]) <> ""), "all", "partial")).
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ All Real Studies Compliant Ingestion Summary ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell

Real studies with IngestionCompliance=all.

ƒ All Real Studies Compliant Calculated 5 hops from ground truth boolean DR-314
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary is flagged all real studies compliant if the real fully compliant count is the real study count.

In plain words

Real fully compliant count equals real study count.

As a formula
= Real Fully Compliant Count = Real Study Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣReal Fully Compliant CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose ingestion compliance is "all". ΣReal Study CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose is synthetic is false() and is control study is false().
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes Ingestion Summary Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Synthetic Study ● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Is Control Study Study

All real studies fully compliant.

ƒ Ingestion Contract Passes Calculated 6 hops from ground truth boolean DR-315
In RuleSpeak

An ingestion summary is flagged ingestion contract passes if all of the following:

    • the all cells valid flag is set
    • the all studies structural flag is set
    • the all real studies compliant flag is set
In plain words

All cells valid, all studies structural, and all real studies compliant.

As a formula
= AND(All Cells Valid, All Studies Structural, All Real Studies Compliant)
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒAll Cells ValidIngestion Summary Valid cell count equals corpus cell count. ƒAll Studies StructuralIngestion Summary Structural compliant count equals study count. ƒAll Real Studies CompliantIngestion Summary Real fully compliant count equals real study count.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Ingestion Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Case Cell ID Case Cell ● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Is Control Study Study ● Is Synthetic Study

Full contract passes.

ƒ Ingestion Witness Note Calculated 7 hops from ground truth string DR-316
In RuleSpeak

The ingestion summary's ingestion witness note is determined by the following priority:

  1. “PASS: ”, followed by the protocol item count, followed by “ contract items; ”, followed by the corpus cell count, followed by “ cells valid; ”, followed by the real fully compliant count, followed by a slash, followed by the real study count, followed by “ real studies fully compliant” if the ingestion contract passes flag is set
  2. “FAIL: ingestion contract violated” in all other cases
In plain words

If ingestion contract passes then "PASS: ", protocol item count, " contract items; ", corpus cell count, " cells valid; ", real fully compliant count, "/", real study count, joined with " real studies fully compliant", otherwise "FAIL: ingestion contract violated".

As a formula
= IF(Ingestion Contract Passes, CONCAT("PASS: ", Protocol Item Count, " contract items; ", Corpus Cell Count, " cells valid; ", Real Fully Compliant Count, "/", Real Study Count, " real studies fully compliant"), "FAIL: ingestion contract violated")
Inputs 5 fields this rule reads
ƒIngestion Contract PassesIngestion Summary All cells valid, all studies structural, and all real studies compliant. ΣProtocol Item CountIngestion Summary Count(protocol ID). ΣCorpus Cell CountIngestion Summary Count(case cell ID). ΣReal Fully Compliant CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose ingestion compliance is "all". ΣReal Study CountIngestion Summary Count of studies whose is synthetic is false() and is control study is false().
Ground truth at the leaves 8 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Cases Case Cell ● Successes Case Cell ● Case Cell ID Case Cell ● Study ID Study ⇢ Study Case Cell ● Is Control Study Study ● Is Synthetic Study ● Protocol ID Ingestion Protocol

PASS/FAIL summary.

1=high priority encode next; 5=blocked/duplicate.

● Stratum Count Estimate Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.
