ƒ Is Ready to Encode Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-317
In RuleSpeak

A candidate study catalog is considered a ready to encode if all of the following:

    • the ingestion status is “candidate”
    • the proposed study ID has a value
    • the citation has a value
    • the stratum variable name has a value
In plain words

Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study ID is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "".

As a formula
= AND(Ingestion Status = "candidate", Proposed Study ID <> "", Citation <> "", Stratum Variable Name <> "")
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected ●Proposed Study IDCandidate Study Catalog Stable slug to assign in Studies when imported. ●CitationCandidate Study Catalog Primary publication citation. ●Stratum Variable NameCandidate Study Catalog Pre-registered stratifying variable name for encoding.
Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Ready to Encode Count Corpus Catalog Summary Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count Corpus Catalog Summary Import Session Ready Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Proposed Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Citation Candidate Study Catalog ● Stratum Variable Name Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when candidate has citation, proposed id, and stratum variable — ready for an import session.

ƒ Is Imported Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-318
In RuleSpeak

A candidate study catalog is considered imported if the ingestion status is “imported”.

In plain words

Ingestion status is "imported".

As a formula
= Ingestion Status = "imported"
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
Consumers 8 direct +7 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected a Count Model Summary ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count Model Summary ƒ Expansion Wave2 Study Count Model Summary ƒ Type Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type Candidate Study Catalog ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Corpus Catalog Summary Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count Corpus Catalog Summary Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Model Summary Domain Diversity Note Model Summary Type Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary Type Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when already in corpus.

↗ Observed Distortion Type Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-319
In RuleSpeak

A candidate study catalog's observed distortion type is the value of LOOKUP(LinkedStudyId, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Linked Study ID, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Linked Study IDCandidate Study Catalog Studies.StudyId when IngestionStatus=imported.
Consumers 3 direct +8 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Expansion Wave1 Economics Expected AD Count Model Summary ƒ Type Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type Candidate Study Catalog Economics Expected a Mismatch Rate Model Summary Type Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Type Prediction Mismatch Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog Type Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

Actual DistortionType from TreatmentRankings when imported.

ƒ Type Prediction Match Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-320
In RuleSpeak

The candidate study catalog's type prediction match is determined by the following priority:

  1. the observed distortion type is the expected distortion type if the imported flag is set
  2. an empty string in all other cases
In plain words

If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(Is Imported, Observed Distortion Type = Expected Distortion Type, "")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]). ●Expected Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
Consumers 2 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Type Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Corpus Catalog Summary Type Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when observed type matches expected (imported rows only).

ƒ Expected Sign Flip Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-321
In RuleSpeak

A candidate study catalog is flagged expected sign flip if all of the following:

    • the expected distortion type is “A”
    • the expected distortion type is “B”
In plain words

Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B".

As a formula
= OR(Expected Distortion Type = "A", Expected Distortion Type = "B")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Expected Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog Hypothesized DistortionType after classification: A, B, C+, C-, D, or unknown.
Consumers 2 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when curator tagged ExpectedDistortionType as A or B (sign-flip expectation).

ƒ Observed Sign Flip Type Calculated 2 hops from ground truth boolean DR-322
In RuleSpeak

The candidate study catalog's observed sign flip type is determined by the following priority:

  1. the OR of the observed distortion type is “A” and the observed distortion type is “B” if the imported flag is set
  2. an empty string in all other cases
In plain words

If is imported then observed distortion type is "A" or observed distortion type is "B", otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(Is Imported, OR(Observed Distortion Type = "A", Observed Distortion Type = "B"), "")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ↗Observed Distortion TypeCandidate Study Catalog LOOKUP({{LinkedStudyId}}, TreatmentRankings[Study], TreatmentRankings[DistortionType]).
Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Candidate Study Catalog Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when observed DistortionType is A or B (imported rows only).

ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-323
In RuleSpeak

The candidate study catalog's sign flip prediction match is determined by the following priority:

  1. the observed sign flip type if all of these hold:
    • the imported flag is set
    • the expected sign flip flag is set
  2. an empty string in all other cases
In plain words

If is imported and expected sign flip then observed sign flip type, otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(AND(Is Imported, Expected Sign Flip), Observed Sign Flip Type, "")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒExpected Sign FlipCandidate Study Catalog Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B". ƒObserved Sign Flip TypeCandidate Study Catalog If is imported then observed distortion type is "A" or observed distortion type is "B", otherwise "".
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Corpus Catalog Summary Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when Expected A∪B and observed is A∪B — weaker flip-type prediction test.

● Data Acquisition Status Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

● Paradox Confirmation Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

confirmed | plausible | pending | not_applicable

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1.

ƒ Is Data Ready Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-324
In RuleSpeak

A candidate study catalog is considered a data ready if all of the following:

    • the data acquisition status is “downloaded”
    • the data acquisition status is “manual_only”
In plain words

Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only".

As a formula
= OR(Data Acquisition Status = "downloaded", Data Acquisition Status = "manual_only")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Data Acquisition StatusCandidate Study Catalog not_started | downloaded | manual_only | blocked
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Data Ready Count Corpus Catalog Summary Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count Corpus Catalog Summary Catalog Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary

TRUE when stratified data is downloaded or available from published tables.

Corpus Catalog Summary

A corpus catalog summary is identified by its name.

Σ Total Catalog Entries Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-325
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's total catalog entries is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count(candidate ID).

As a formula
= COUNT(Candidate ID)
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Candidate IDCandidate Study Catalog Unique slug for this catalog entry.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Candidate ID Candidate Study Catalog

All catalog rows. Fixed loop-90 (see IngestionSummary.ProtocolItemCount for why COUNTIFS(...,"<>") is unsafe).

Σ Imported Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-326
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's imported count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "imported".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "imported")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one

A corpus catalog summary's candidate count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

As a formula

Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one

In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's blocked count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Inputs 1 fields this rule reads

Σ Ready to Encode Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-329
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's ready to encode count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose is ready to encode is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Ready to Encode, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Ready to EncodeCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study ID is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Import Session Ready Corpus Catalog Summary ƒ Catalog Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Proposed Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Citation Candidate Study Catalog ● Stratum Variable Name Candidate Study Catalog

Candidates passing IsReadyToEncode.

Σ High Priority Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-330
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's high priority count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's high priorities.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "candidate" and priority is 1 plus count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "candidate" and priority is 2.

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "candidate", Priority, 1) + COUNTIFS(Ingestion Status, "candidate", Priority, 2)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected ●PriorityCandidate Study Catalog 1=high priority encode next; 5=blocked/duplicate.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Priority Candidate Study Catalog

Candidates with Priority <= 2.

ƒ Import Session Ready Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-331
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary is flagged import session ready if all of the following:

    • the candidate count is at least 20
    • the ready to encode count is at least 15
In plain words

Candidate count is at least 20 and ready to encode count is at least 15.

As a formula
= AND(Candidate Count >= 20, Ready to Encode Count >= 15)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCandidate CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "candidate". ΣReady to Encode CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose is ready to encode is true().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Catalog Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Proposed Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Citation Candidate Study Catalog ● Stratum Variable Name Candidate Study Catalog

TRUE when backlog is large enough for a bulk import session.

Σ Type Prediction Match Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-332
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's type prediction match count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose type prediction match is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Type Prediction Match, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒType Prediction MatchCandidate Study Catalog If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Type Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Imported catalog rows where ObservedDistortionType exactly matches ExpectedDistortionType.

Σ Type Prediction Mismatch Count Aggregation 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-333
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's type prediction mismatch count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are not type prediction match.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose is imported is true() and type prediction match is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Imported, TRUE(), Type Prediction Match, FALSE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒType Prediction MatchCandidate Study Catalog If is imported then observed distortion type equals expected distortion type, otherwise "".
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Imported catalog rows where exact type prediction failed.

Σ Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-334
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction eligible count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are expected sign flip.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Imported, TRUE(), Expected Sign Flip, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs ImportedCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "imported". ƒExpected Sign FlipCandidate Study Catalog Expected distortion type is "A" or expected distortion type is "B".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Imported rows where ExpectedDistortionType was A or B.

Σ Sign Flip Prediction Match Count Aggregation 4 hops from ground truth integer DR-335
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction match count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose sign flip prediction match is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Sign Flip Prediction Match, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
ƒSign Flip Prediction MatchCandidate Study Catalog If is imported and expected sign flip then observed sign flip type, otherwise "".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Corpus Catalog Summary ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

Expected A∪B rows where observed type is also A∪B.

ƒ Type Prediction Match Rate Calculated 4 hops from ground truth number DR-336
In RuleSpeak

The corpus catalog summary's type prediction match rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the imported count is 0
  2. the type prediction match count divided by the imported count in all other cases
In plain words

If imported count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type prediction match count divided by imported count).

As a formula
= IF(Imported Count = 0, "", Type Prediction Match Count / Imported Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣImported CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "imported". ΣType Prediction Match CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose type prediction match is true().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Exact-match rate across imported catalog rows.

ƒ Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate Calculated 5 hops from ground truth number DR-337
In RuleSpeak

The corpus catalog summary's sign flip prediction match rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the sign flip prediction eligible count is 0
  2. the sign flip prediction match count divided by the sign flip prediction eligible count in all other cases
In plain words

If sign flip prediction eligible count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sign flip prediction match count divided by sign flip prediction eligible count).

As a formula
= IF(Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count = 0, "", Sign Flip Prediction Match Count / Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣSign Flip Prediction Eligible CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true(). ΣSign Flip Prediction Match CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose sign flip prediction match is true().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog

Sign-flip-type match rate among Expected A∪B rows.

ƒ Catalog Witness Note Calculated 4 hops from ground truth string DR-338
In RuleSpeak

The corpus catalog summary's catalog witness note is determined by the following priority:

  1. “READY: ”, followed by the ready to encode count, followed by “ encode-ready (”, followed by the data ready count, followed by “ data-ready) of ”, followed by the candidate count, followed by “ candidates” if the import session ready flag is set
  2. “BUILDING: ”, followed by the candidate count, followed by “ candidates — ”, followed by the data ready count, followed by “ data-ready, ”, followed by the ready to encode count, followed by “ encode-ready” in all other cases
In plain words

If import session ready then "READY: ", ready to encode count, " encode-ready (", data ready count, " data-ready) of ", candidate count, joined with " candidates", otherwise "BUILDING: ", candidate count, " candidates — ", data ready count, " data-ready, ", ready to encode count, joined with " encode-ready".

As a formula
= IF(Import Session Ready, CONCAT("READY: ", Ready to Encode Count, " encode-ready (", Data Ready Count, " data-ready) of ", Candidate Count, " candidates"), CONCAT("BUILDING: ", Candidate Count, " candidates — ", Data Ready Count, " data-ready, ", Ready to Encode Count, " encode-ready"))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ƒImport Session ReadyCorpus Catalog Summary Candidate count is at least 20 and ready to encode count is at least 15. ΣReady to Encode CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose is ready to encode is true(). ΣData Ready CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose is data ready is true() and ingestion status is "candidate". ΣCandidate CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "candidate".
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Proposed Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Citation Candidate Study Catalog ● Stratum Variable Name Candidate Study Catalog ● Data Acquisition Status Candidate Study Catalog

Human-readable readiness summary.

ƒ Catalog Prediction Witness Note Calculated 6 hops from ground truth string DR-339
In RuleSpeak ⚠︎ mechanical

A corpus catalog summary's catalog prediction witness note is computed as “exact=”, followed by the type prediction match count, followed by a slash, followed by the imported count, followed by “ (”, followed by the type prediction match rate times 100 rounded to 1 decimal place(s), followed by “%); flipPred=”, followed by the sign flip prediction match count, followed by a slash, followed by the sign flip prediction eligible count, followed by “ (”, followed by the sign flip prediction match rate times 100 rounded to 1 decimal place(s), followed by “%)”.

In plain words

"exact=", type prediction match count, "/", imported count, " (", (type prediction match rate times 100) rounded to 1 decimal places, "%); flipPred=", sign flip prediction match count, "/", sign flip prediction eligible count, " (", (sign flip prediction match rate times 100) rounded to 1 decimal places, joined with "%)".

As a formula
= CONCAT("exact=", Type Prediction Match Count, "/", Imported Count, " (", ROUND(Type Prediction Match Rate * 100, 1), "%); flipPred=", Sign Flip Prediction Match Count, "/", Sign Flip Prediction Eligible Count, " (", ROUND(Sign Flip Prediction Match Rate * 100, 1), "%)")
Inputs 6 fields this rule reads
ΣType Prediction Match CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose type prediction match is true(). ΣImported CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose ingestion status is "imported". ƒType Prediction Match RateCorpus Catalog Summary If imported count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type prediction match count divided by imported count). ΣSign Flip Prediction Match CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose sign flip prediction match is true(). ΣSign Flip Prediction Eligible CountCorpus Catalog Summary Count of candidate study catalog whose is imported is true() and expected sign flip is true(). ƒSign Flip Prediction Match RateCorpus Catalog Summary If sign flip prediction eligible count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sign flip prediction match count divided by sign flip prediction eligible count).
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Linked Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Expected Distortion Type Candidate Study Catalog

Loop-65 type-prediction audit summary.

Σ Data Ready Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-340
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's data ready count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that have a ingestion status of “candidate”.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose is data ready is true() and ingestion status is "candidate".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Data Ready, TRUE(), Ingestion Status, "candidate")
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Data ReadyCandidate Study Catalog Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only". ●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Catalog Witness Note Corpus Catalog Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Data Acquisition Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog

Candidates with IsDataReady=TRUE.

Σ Encode Pipeline Ready Count Aggregation 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-341
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's encode pipeline ready count is the number of the corpus catalog summary's candidate study catalog that are data readies.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose is ready to encode is true() and is data ready is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Ready to Encode, TRUE(), Is Data Ready, TRUE())
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒIs Ready to EncodeCandidate Study Catalog Ingestion status is "candidate", proposed study ID is not "", citation is not "", and stratum variable name is not "". ƒIs Data ReadyCandidate Study Catalog Data acquisition status is "downloaded" or data acquisition status is "manual_only".
Ground truth at the leaves 5 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Proposed Study ID Candidate Study Catalog ● Citation Candidate Study Catalog ● Stratum Variable Name Candidate Study Catalog ● Data Acquisition Status Candidate Study Catalog

Candidates both data-ready and encode-ready.

Σ Expansion Candidate Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-342
In RuleSpeak

A corpus catalog summary's expansion candidate count is the number of candidate study catalog related to the corpus catalog summary.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose expansion wave is "expansion-wave-1".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Expansion Wave, "expansion-wave-1")
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Expansion WaveCandidate Study Catalog Catalog wave tag; loop-67 expansion rows = expansion-wave-1.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Expansion Wave Candidate Study Catalog

Loop-67 expansion-wave-1 candidates.

Domain Expansion Target

A domain expansion target is identified by its name.

● Domain Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Current Imported Count Domain Expansion Target Σ Candidate Queued Count Domain Expansion Target Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target

Domain tag matching Studies.Domain.

● Legacy Domain Alias Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct +3 transitive rules that read this one
Σ Current Imported Count Domain Expansion Target Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target

Studies.Domain slug for legacy imported corpus rows.

● Target Min Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Gap Count Domain Expansion Target ƒ Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target

Minimum real studies desired in this domain.

Σ Current Imported Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-343
In RuleSpeak

A domain expansion target's current imported count is the number of the domain expansion target's studies that are not synthetic.

In plain words

Count of studies whose domain is if legacy domain alias is not "" then legacy domain alias, otherwise domain and is synthetic is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Domain, IF(Legacy Domain Alias<>"", Legacy Domain Alias, Domain), Is Synthetic, FALSE())
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
●DomainStudy Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification. ●Legacy Domain AliasDomain Expansion Target Studies.Domain slug for legacy imported corpus rows. ●DomainDomain Expansion Target Domain tag matching Studies.Domain. ●Is SyntheticStudy TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target
Ground truth at the leaves 4 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Legacy Domain Alias Domain Expansion Target ● Domain Domain Expansion Target ● Is Synthetic Study

Real studies in corpus matching LegacyDomainAlias (or Domain when no alias).

Σ Candidate Queued Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-344
In RuleSpeak

A domain expansion target's candidate queued count is the number of the domain expansion target's candidate study catalog that have a ingestion status of “candidate”.

In plain words

Count of candidate study catalog whose domain is domain and ingestion status is "candidate".

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Domain, Domain, Ingestion Status, "candidate")
Inputs 3 fields this rule reads
●DomainCandidate Study Catalog Domain tag: medicine, epidemiology, legal, sports, education, economics, social-science. ●DomainDomain Expansion Target Domain tag matching Studies.Domain. ●Ingestion StatusCandidate Study Catalog imported | candidate | blocked | rejected
Consumers 1 direct +2 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Projected Count Domain Expansion Target Gap Count Domain Expansion Target Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target
Ground truth at the leaves 3 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Domain Domain Expansion Target ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog

Catalog candidates in this domain.

ƒ Projected Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-345
In RuleSpeak

A domain expansion target's projected count is computed as the current imported count plus the candidate queued count.

In plain words

Current imported count plus candidate queued count.

As a formula
= Current Imported Count + Candidate Queued Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣCurrent Imported CountDomain Expansion Target Count of studies whose domain is if legacy domain alias is not "" then legacy domain alias, otherwise domain and is synthetic is false(). ΣCandidate Queued CountDomain Expansion Target Count of candidate study catalog whose domain is domain and ingestion status is "candidate".
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Gap Count Domain Expansion Target ƒ Is Under Represented Domain Expansion Target
Ground truth at the leaves 6 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Legacy Domain Alias Domain Expansion Target ● Domain Domain Expansion Target ● Is Synthetic Study ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog

Current + queued candidates.

ƒ Gap Count Calculated 3 hops from ground truth integer DR-346
In RuleSpeak

A domain expansion target's gap count is computed as the largest of 0 and the target min count minus the projected count.

In plain words

The largest of 0 and (target min count minus projected count).

As a formula
= MAX(0, Target Min Count - Projected Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Target Min CountDomain Expansion Target Minimum real studies desired in this domain. ƒProjected CountDomain Expansion Target Current imported count plus candidate queued count.
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Target Min Count Domain Expansion Target ● Domain Study ● Legacy Domain Alias Domain Expansion Target ● Domain Domain Expansion Target ● Is Synthetic Study ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog

Studies still needed to hit target after queued imports.

ƒ Is Under Represented Calculated 3 hops from ground truth boolean DR-347
In RuleSpeak

A domain expansion target is considered under-represented if the projected count is less than the target min count.

In plain words

Projected count is less than target min count.

As a formula
= Projected Count < Target Min Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒProjected CountDomain Expansion Target Current imported count plus candidate queued count. ●Target Min CountDomain Expansion Target Minimum real studies desired in this domain.
Ground truth at the leaves 7 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Domain Study ● Legacy Domain Alias Domain Expansion Target ● Domain Domain Expansion Target ● Is Synthetic Study ● Domain Candidate Study Catalog ● Ingestion Status Candidate Study Catalog ● Target Min Count Domain Expansion Target

TRUE when projected count still below target.

Study Import Template

A study import template is identified by its name.

● Sort Order Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Order to execute during import.

● Target Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Rulebook table to write.

● Row Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

What rows to create.

● Required Fields Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Key columns that must be populated.

● Mechanical Check Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

How to verify step complete.

Sweep Study Config

A sweep study config is identified by its name.

● Sweep Stratum Label Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Stratum whose A/B allocation is swept (highest |AllocationBias| in original data).

● N Sweep Stratum Total Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Total cases in the sweep stratum (fixed across sweep rows).

● Sweep Rate a Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Success rate for treatment A in the sweep stratum.

● Sweep Rate B Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Success rate for treatment B in the sweep stratum.

● N Fixed a Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

A cases in all non-swept strata (fixed).

● N Fixed B Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

B cases in all non-swept strata (fixed).

● Fixed Rate a Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Weighted success rate for A in fixed strata.

● Fixed Rate B Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Weighted success rate for B in fixed strata.

● Original Alloc Fraction a Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Observed fraction of sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A.

● Sweep Corrected Gap Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Allocation-free gap: sum_s (n_s/N)*(rate_As-rate_Bs). Invariant under within-stratum reallocation.

Substrate Conformance Field

A substrate conformance field is identified by its name.

● Source Table Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TreatmentRankings | StratumSummaries | StratumVariables

● Field Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

PascalCase rulebook field name.

● Pg Column Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

snake_case vw_* column.

● Owl Local Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

camelCase OWL property local name.

● Data Type Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

float | int | bool | str

● Assert From Postgres Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TRUE when OWL cannot derive this field yet and Postgres value is asserted before SHACL fixpoint.

● In Compare Set Ground truth boolean

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype boolean); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

TRUE when reason.py diffs this field between substrates.

Discovery Hypothes

A discovery hypothes is identified by its name.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Pass criterion in plain language.

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

LoopId where this hypothesis was registered.

● Epistemic Tier Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

consistency-check (definition-linked regression) | corpus-hypothesis (contingent corpus pattern)

Discovery Finding

A discovery finding is identified by its name.

↗ Hypothesis Statement Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-348
In RuleSpeak

A discovery finding's hypothesis statement is the value of LOOKUP(HypothesisId, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{HypothesisId}}, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Hypothesis ID, DiscoveryHypotheses[HypothesisId], DiscoveryHypotheses[Statement])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Hypothesis IDDiscovery Finding FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding

Lookup: DiscoveryHypotheses.Statement.

ƒ Observed Metric Calculated 1 hop from ground truth string DR-349
In RuleSpeak

A discovery finding's observed metric is computed as the value of IF(HypothesisId = "H-latent-d", CONCAT("fraction=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-purity", CONCAT("maxPurity=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-small-effect", CONCAT("stable=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]), " latent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-zero", CONCAT("flips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-dist", CONCAT("epi=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]), " edu=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-manifest", CONCAT("confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-latent", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), " collLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]), " collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-confounder", CONCAT("ExplainedConfounderCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), ", ConfounderSignFlipCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", CONCAT("ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-exact-match", CONCAT("exactRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", CONCAT("flipPredRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", CONCAT("econHighImbFlips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]), "; epiRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; legal=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LegalHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; sports=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsHighImbalanceSignFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-cplus-magnitude", CONCAT("C+=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]), " C-=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), " D=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-encoding-selection", CONCAT("EconExpectedAMismatchRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-profiles-stable", CONCAT("eduLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]), " sportsLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]), " econFlipRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", CONCAT("maxRange=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]), " fails=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-bidirectional", CONCAT("explained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), " confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " falsePos=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]), " unexplained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", CONCAT("collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-theorem-portfolio", CONCAT("theoremCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", CONCAT("rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]), ", n=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-identity-map-coverage", CONCAT("coverage=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", CONCAT("medicine_flip_rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", CONCAT("epi_manifest_count=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-mechanism-other-drain", CONCAT("other_count=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", CONCAT("manifest=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]), ", n=", LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", CONCAT("rate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", CONCAT("type_d_frac=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", CONCAT("unexplained_confounder_flips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-confounder-drift-type-d", CONCAT("severity=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]), " geography=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]), " situational=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]), " age=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeDriftDirection]), " institutional=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[InstitutionalDriftDirection]), " socioeconomic=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SocioeconomicDriftDirection])), ""))))))))))))))))))))))))))))).

In plain words

IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", CONCAT("fraction=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", CONCAT("maxPurity=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", CONCAT("stable=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]), " latent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-zero", CONCAT("flips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-dist", CONCAT("epi=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]), " edu=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-manifest", CONCAT("confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-latent", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), " collLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Hypothesis IDDiscovery Finding FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Evidence Discovery Finding
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding

Witnessed metric value from ModelSummary for this hypothesis.

ƒ Is Confirmed Calculated 1 hop from ground truth boolean DR-350
In RuleSpeak

A discovery finding is considered confirmed if the value of IF(HypothesisId = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-zero", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-dist", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion]), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-causal-latent", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]) >= 5), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-confounder", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-exact-match", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]) > 0.15), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-cplus-magnitude", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF(HypothesisId = "H-econ-encoding-selection", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate]) > 0.5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-domain-profiles-stable", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate]) < 0.05), IF(HypothesisId = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]) < 0.0001, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount]) = 0), IF(HypothesisId = "H-explained-bidirectional", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-theorem-portfolio", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount]) >= 4, IF(HypothesisId = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.40, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount]) >= 10), IF(HypothesisId = "H-identity-map-coverage", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate]) >= 0.95, IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.45, IF(HypothesisId = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-mechanism-other-drain", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) <= 5, IF(HypothesisId = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF(HypothesisId = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate]) <= 0.15, LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 5), IF(HypothesisId = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction]) >= 0.25, LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF(HypothesisId = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF(HypothesisId = "H-confounder-drift-type-d", AND(OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[InstitutionalDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SocioeconomicDriftDirection]) <> "rising"), FALSE())))))))))))))))))))))))))))))).

In plain words

IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-zero", "H-causal-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]) >= 5), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-confounder", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-exact-match", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]) > 0.15), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-cplus-magnitude", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-encoding-selection", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-profiles-stable", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]) > 0.5, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate]) < 0.05), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]) < 0.0001, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount]) = 0), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-bidirectional", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-theorem-portfolio", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount]) >= 4, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-age-identity-flip-rate", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.40, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeIdentityStudyCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-identity-map-coverage", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[IdentityMapCoverageRate]) >= 0.95, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-severity-medicine-manifest", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityMedicineManifestFlipRate]) >= 0.45, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-severity-epi-latent-only", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityEpiManifestFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-mechanism-other-drain", LOOKUP("cluster-id-mechanism-other", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) <= 5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-selection-frailty-zero-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[ManifestFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("cluster-id-selection-frailty", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-identity-low-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderIdentityManifestFlipRate]) <= 0.15, LOOKUP("cluster-id-collider-proxy", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 5), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction]) >= 0.25, LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-confounder-drift-type-d", AND(OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[InstitutionalDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SocioeconomicDriftDirection]) <> "rising"), FALSE())))))))))))))))))))))))))))))).

= IF(Hypothesis ID = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF(Hypothesis ID = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF(Hypothesis ID = "H-econ-zero", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 5), IF(Hypothesis ID = "H-geographic-stable-type-d", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicTypeDFraction]) >= 0.25, LOOKUP("cluster-id-geographic-composition", IdentityClusterSummaries[IdentityClusterId], IdentityClusterSummaries[StudyCount]) >= 3), IF(Hypothesis ID = "H-unexplained-flips-only-nonconfounders", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF(Hypothesis ID = ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SeverityDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[GeographicDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), OR(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SituationalDriftDirection]) = "single-decade"), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AgeDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[InstitutionalDriftDirection]) <> "rising", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SocioeconomicDriftDirection]) <> "rising"), FALSE()))))))))))))))))))))))))))))))
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Hypothesis IDDiscovery Finding FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.
Consumers 2 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Corpus Pattern Superseded Fail Count Model Summary ƒ Evidence Discovery Finding Expansion Wave3 Discovery Note Model Summary
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding

TRUE when the witnessed metric satisfies the pre-registered ExpectedOutcome.

ƒ Evidence Calculated 2 hops from ground truth string DR-351
In RuleSpeak

The discovery finding's evidence is determined by the following priority:

  1. “PASS: ”, followed by the observed metric if the is confirmed is true
  2. “FAIL: ”, followed by the observed metric if the is confirmed is false
  3. an empty string in all other cases
In plain words

If is confirmed equals true() then "PASS: " joined with observed metric, otherwise if is confirmed equals false() then "FAIL: " joined with observed metric, otherwise "".

As a formula
= IF(Is Confirmed = TRUE(), CONCAT("PASS: ", Observed Metric), IF(Is Confirmed = FALSE(), CONCAT("FAIL: ", Observed Metric), ""))
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads

Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Hypothesis ID Discovery Finding

Pass/fail witness string for UI and conclusions.

● Witnessed in Loop Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

LoopId where this finding was witnessed.

Corpus Domain

A corpus domain is identified by its name.

● Display Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable domain name.

● Expansion Target Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Target study count from corpus-expansion-plan.md.

● Legacy Studies Domain Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Studies.Domain alias for counting already-imported corpus rows.

● Plan Source Section Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Section heading in corpus-expansion-plan.md.

Confounder Identity

A confounder identity is identified by its name.

● Display Name Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Human-readable archetype name.

● Mechanism Class Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Mechanism taxonomy token.

● Description Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Narrative description of confounding mechanism.

● Policy Default Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Default analytic policy for this confounding archetype.

Σ Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-352
In RuleSpeak

A confounder identity's study count is the number of the confounder identity's studies.

In plain words

Count(map ID, confounder identity, confounder identity ID).

As a formula
= COUNT(Map ID, ConfounderIdentity, Confounder Identity ID)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Map IDStratum Variable Identity Map Unique slug. ●Confounder Identity IDConfounder Identity Unique slug (e.g. id-age-composition).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Map ID Stratum Variable Identity Map ● Confounder Identity ID Confounder Identity

Studies with a StratumVariable mapped to this identity.

Stratum Variable Identity Map

A stratum variable identity map is identified by its name and is related to a stratum variable and a confounder identity.

⇢ Stratum Variable Relationship string

A pointer to Stratum Variable. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

FK → StratumVariables.

⇢ Confounder Identity Relationship string

A pointer to Confounder Identity. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

FK → ConfounderIdentities.

● Variable Name Normalized Ground truth string

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype string); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Lowercase normalized variable token for dedup audit.

Identity Cluster Summary

An identity cluster summary is identified by its name and is related to a confounder identity.

⇢ Confounder Identity Relationship string

A pointer to Confounder Identity. It is not derived from anything else — it is the link the rest of the DAG hangs off.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
↗ Display Name Identity Cluster Summary

FK → ConfounderIdentities.

↗ Display Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-353
In RuleSpeak

An identity cluster summary's display name is the value of LOOKUP(ConfounderIdentity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{ConfounderIdentity}}, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Confounder Identity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢Confounder IdentityIdentity Cluster Summary FK → ConfounderIdentities.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on

Lookup archetype display name.

● Study Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.
