Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Manifest Flip Rate Identity Cluster Summary

IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cluster.

● Latent Flip Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

LatentFlipPotential=TRUE without manifest flip.

● Type D Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Type-D studies in cluster.

● Avg Allocation Distortion Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Mean allocation distortion in cluster.

● Latent Fraction Among Type D Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Fraction of Type-D rows with LatentFlipPotential.

● Domain Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Distinct Studies.Domain values in cluster.

ƒ Manifest Flip Rate Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-354
In RuleSpeak

The identity cluster summary's manifest flip rate is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if the study count is 0
  2. the manifest flip count divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (manifest flip count divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(Study Count=0, "", Manifest Flip Count / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study CountIdentity Cluster Summary Real studies in cluster (customize SQL). ●Manifest Flip CountIdentity Cluster Summary IsSignFlip=TRUE count in cluster.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study Count Identity Cluster Summary ● Manifest Flip Count Identity Cluster Summary

ManifestFlipCount / StudyCount.

↗ Identity Display Name Confounder Distortion Timeline ↗ Mechanism Class Confounder Distortion Timeline Σ Identity Earliest Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline Σ Identity Latest Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline ↗ Identity Earliest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline ↗ Identity Latest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline

FK -> ConfounderIdentities.

Σ Identity Earliest Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline Σ Identity Latest Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline Identity Earliest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline Identity Latest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline

Studies.ValidTimeDecade bucket this cell aggregates over (e.g. 1980 covers valid-time midpoints 1980-1989).

● Study Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 4 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline ƒ Sign Flip Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline ƒ Mean Signal Purity Confounder Distortion Timeline ƒ Mean Allocation Distortion Confounder Distortion Timeline

Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()).

● Type D Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline

Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.DistortionType = 'D' (clean, non-reversing).

● Sign Flip Count Ground truth integer

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype integer); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Sign Flip Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline

Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.IsSignFlip = TRUE.

● Sum Signal Purity Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Mean Signal Purity Confounder Distortion Timeline

Sum of TreatmentRankings.SignalPurity across cell studies (numerator for MeanSignalPurity; summed rather than pre-averaged so the aggregate survives refresh re-runs without drift).

● Sum Allocation Distortion Ground truth number

Ground truth. Written directly to the database (datatype number); not computed from anything else. Everything derived ultimately traces back to values like this one.

Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Mean Allocation Distortion Confounder Distortion Timeline

Sum of TreatmentRankings.AllocationDistortion across cell studies (numerator for MeanAllocationDistortion).

ƒ Type D Fraction Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-357
In RuleSpeak

The confounder distortion timeline's type d fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the study count is blank
    • the study count is 0
  2. the type d count divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count is "" or study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (type d count divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(OR(Study Count = "", Study Count = 0), "", Type D Count / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()). ●Type D CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.DistortionType = 'D' (clean, non-reversing).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study Count Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Type D Count Confounder Distortion Timeline

TypeDCount / StudyCount.

ƒ Sign Flip Fraction Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-358
In RuleSpeak

The confounder distortion timeline's sign flip fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the study count is blank
    • the study count is 0
  2. the sign flip count divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count is "" or study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sign flip count divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(OR(Study Count = "", Study Count = 0), "", Sign Flip Count / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()). ●Sign Flip CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.IsSignFlip = TRUE.
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study Count Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Sign Flip Count Confounder Distortion Timeline

SignFlipCount / StudyCount.

ƒ Mean Signal Purity Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-359
In RuleSpeak

The confounder distortion timeline's mean signal purity is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the study count is blank
    • the study count is 0
  2. the sum signal purity divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count is "" or study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sum signal purity divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(OR(Study Count = "", Study Count = 0), "", Sum Signal Purity / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()). ●Sum Signal PurityConfounder Distortion Timeline Sum of TreatmentRankings.SignalPurity across cell studies (numerator for MeanSignalPurity; summed rather than pre-averaged so the aggregate survives refresh re-runs without drift).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study Count Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Sum Signal Purity Confounder Distortion Timeline

SumSignalPurity / StudyCount.

ƒ Mean Allocation Distortion Calculated 1 hop from ground truth number DR-360
In RuleSpeak

The confounder distortion timeline's mean allocation distortion is determined by the following priority:

  1. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the study count is blank
    • the study count is 0
  2. the sum allocation distortion divided by the study count in all other cases
In plain words

If study count is "" or study count equals 0 then "", otherwise (sum allocation distortion divided by study count).

As a formula
= IF(OR(Study Count = "", Study Count = 0), "", Sum Allocation Distortion / Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Study CountConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()). ●Sum Allocation DistortionConfounder Distortion Timeline Sum of TreatmentRankings.AllocationDistortion across cell studies (numerator for MeanAllocationDistortion).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Study Count Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Sum Allocation Distortion Confounder Distortion Timeline

SumAllocationDistortion / StudyCount.

↗ Identity Display Name Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-361
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's identity display name is the value of LOOKUP(ConfounderIdentity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{ConfounderIdentity}}, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Confounder Identity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[DisplayName])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline

Lookup: ConfounderIdentities.DisplayName via ConfounderIdentity.

↗ Mechanism Class Lookup 1 hop from ground truth string DR-362
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's mechanism class is the value of LOOKUP(ConfounderIdentity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[MechanismClass]).

In plain words

LOOKUP({{ConfounderIdentity}}, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[MechanismClass]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(Confounder Identity, ConfounderIdentities[ConfounderIdentityId], ConfounderIdentities[MechanismClass])
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities.
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline

Lookup: ConfounderIdentities.MechanismClass via ConfounderIdentity — the archetype group used to test whether Type-D drift is confounder-specific.

Σ Identity Earliest Decade Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-363
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's identity earliest decade is the smallest valid time decade across the confounder distortion timeline related to the confounder distortion timeline.

In plain words

Smallest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity.

As a formula
= MINIFS(Valid Time Decade, Confounder Identity, Confounder Identity)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Valid Time DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies.ValidTimeDecade bucket this cell aggregates over (e.g. 1980 covers valid-time midpoints 1980-1989). ⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities.
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
↗ Identity Earliest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline ƒ Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Valid Time Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline ⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline

Earliest ValidTimeDecade present in this table for this ConfounderIdentity.

Σ Identity Latest Decade Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-364
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's identity latest decade is the largest valid time decade across the confounder distortion timeline related to the confounder distortion timeline.

In plain words

Largest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity.

As a formula
= MAXIFS(Valid Time Decade, Confounder Identity, Confounder Identity)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
●Valid Time DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Studies.ValidTimeDecade bucket this cell aggregates over (e.g. 1980 covers valid-time midpoints 1980-1989). ⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities.
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
↗ Identity Latest Type D Fraction Confounder Distortion Timeline ƒ Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Valid Time Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline ⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline

Latest ValidTimeDecade present in this table for this ConfounderIdentity.

↗ Identity Earliest Type D Fraction Lookup 2 hops from ground truth number DR-365
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's identity earliest type d fraction is the value of LOOKUP(CONCAT(ConfounderIdentity, "-x-", IdentityEarliestDecade), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]).

In plain words

LOOKUP(CONCAT({{ConfounderIdentity}}, "-x-", {{IdentityEarliestDecade}}), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(CONCAT(Confounder Identity, "-x-", Identity Earliest Decade), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction])
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities. ΣIdentity Earliest DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Smallest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Valid Time Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline

TypeDFraction of this ConfounderIdentity's earliest decade cell -- the start point for the drift comparison.

↗ Identity Latest Type D Fraction Lookup 2 hops from ground truth number DR-366
In RuleSpeak

A confounder distortion timeline's identity latest type d fraction is the value of LOOKUP(CONCAT(ConfounderIdentity, "-x-", IdentityLatestDecade), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]).

In plain words

LOOKUP(CONCAT({{ConfounderIdentity}}, "-x-", {{IdentityLatestDecade}}), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]).

As a formula
= LOOKUP(CONCAT(Confounder Identity, "-x-", Identity Latest Decade), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction])
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
⇢Confounder IdentityConfounder Distortion Timeline FK -> ConfounderIdentities. ΣIdentity Latest DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Largest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity.
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Drift Direction Confounder Distortion Timeline
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline ● Valid Time Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline

TypeDFraction of this ConfounderIdentity's latest decade cell -- the end point for the drift comparison.

ƒ Drift Direction Calculated 3 hops from ground truth string DR-367
In RuleSpeak

The confounder distortion timeline's drift direction is determined by the following priority:

  1. “single-decade” if the identity earliest decade is the identity latest decade
  2. an empty string if any of these hold:
    • the identity earliest type d fraction is blank
    • the identity latest type d fraction is blank
  3. “rising” if the identity latest type d fraction is greater than the identity earliest type d fraction plus 0.05
  4. “falling” if the identity latest type d fraction is less than the identity earliest type d fraction minus 0.05
  5. “flat” in all other cases
In plain words

If identity earliest decade equals identity latest decade then "single-decade", otherwise if identity earliest type d fraction is "" or identity latest type d fraction is "" then "", otherwise if identity latest type d fraction is greater than (identity earliest type d fraction plus 0.05) then "rising", otherwise if identity latest type d fraction is less than (identity earliest type d fraction minus 0.05) then "falling", otherwise "flat".

As a formula
= IF(Identity Earliest Decade = Identity Latest Decade, "single-decade", IF(OR(Identity Earliest Type D Fraction = "", Identity Latest Type D Fraction = ""), "", IF(Identity Latest Type D Fraction > Identity Earliest Type D Fraction + 0.05, "rising", IF(Identity Latest Type D Fraction < Identity Earliest Type D Fraction - 0.05, "falling", "flat"))))
Inputs 4 fields this rule reads
ΣIdentity Earliest DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Smallest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity. ΣIdentity Latest DecadeConfounder Distortion Timeline Largest valid time decade across all confounder distortion timeline whose confounder identity is confounder identity. ↗Identity Earliest Type D FractionConfounder Distortion Timeline LOOKUP(CONCAT({{ConfounderIdentity}}, "-x-", {{IdentityEarliestDecade}}), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]). ↗Identity Latest Type D FractionConfounder Distortion Timeline LOOKUP(CONCAT({{ConfounderIdentity}}, "-x-", {{IdentityLatestDecade}}), ConfounderDistortionTimeline[CellId], ConfounderDistortionTimeline[TypeDFraction]).
Ground truth at the leaves 2 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Valid Time Decade Confounder Distortion Timeline ⇢ Confounder Identity Confounder Distortion Timeline

rising | falling | flat | single-decade -- compares this ConfounderIdentity's earliest-decade TypeDFraction to its latest-decade TypeDFraction across the whole table (same value on every row for a given ConfounderIdentity by construction).

Corpus Balance

A corpus balance is identified by its name.

ƒ Total Study Count Calculated 2 hops from ground truth integer DR-368
In RuleSpeak

A corpus balance's total study count is computed as the simpson study count plus the control study count.

In plain words

Simpson study count plus control study count.

As a formula
= Simpson Study Count + Control Study Count
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ΣSimpson Study CountCorpus Balance Count of studies whose is control study is false(). ΣControl Study CountCorpus Balance Count of studies whose is control study is true().
Consumers 1 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Control Fraction Corpus Balance
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Control Study Study

Total studies in corpus (Simpson + control). Fixed loop-90: was authored with COUNTA/bareword refs and an uppercase Formula key, which the transpiler cannot parse. COUNTIFS(...,"<>") also mistranslates (known transpiler bug: literal '<>' string instead of IS NOT NULL), so this is expressed as SimpsonStudyCount + ControlStudyCount instead.

Σ Simpson Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-369
In RuleSpeak

A corpus balance's simpson study count is the number of studies related to the corpus balance.

In plain words

Count of studies whose is control study is false().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Control Study, FALSE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Is Control StudyStudy TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.
Consumers 1 direct +1 transitive rules that read this one
ƒ Total Study Count Corpus Balance Control Fraction Corpus Balance
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Control Study Study

Studies where IsControlStudy=FALSE. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount).

Σ Control Study Count Aggregation 1 hop from ground truth integer DR-370
In RuleSpeak

A corpus balance's control study count is the number of studies related to the corpus balance.

In plain words

Count of studies whose is control study is true().

As a formula
= COUNTIFS(Is Control Study, TRUE())
Inputs 1 fields this rule reads
●Is Control StudyStudy TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.
Consumers 2 direct rules that read this one
ƒ Total Study Count Corpus Balance ƒ Control Fraction Corpus Balance
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Control Study Study

Studies where IsControlStudy=TRUE. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount).

ƒ Control Fraction Calculated 3 hops from ground truth number DR-371
In RuleSpeak

The corpus balance's control fraction is determined by the following priority:

  1. 0 if the total study count is 0
  2. the control study count divided by the total study count in all other cases
In plain words

If total study count equals 0 then 0, otherwise (control study count divided by total study count).

As a formula
= IF(Total Study Count = 0, 0, Control Study Count / Total Study Count)
Inputs 2 fields this rule reads
ƒTotal Study CountCorpus Balance Simpson study count plus control study count. ΣControl Study CountCorpus Balance Count of studies whose is control study is true().
Ground truth at the leaves 1 the raw values this ultimately depends on
● Is Control Study Study

ControlStudyCount / TotalStudyCount. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount); returns 0 for the degenerate empty-corpus edge instead of "" (see TreatmentRankings.DistortionRatio for why IF(x=0,"",...) is unsafe on a numeric-typed field).

Rendered from the rulebook by the shared RuleSpeak engine (rulebook-rulespeak-core). The same engine narrates the React Explainer DAG, so every narration mode reads identically across both.

● Ground truth ƒ Calculated Σ Aggregation ↗ Lookup ⇢ Relationship
